Text: I bought this thing after a recommendation from a pet store rep. My dog hardly touched the stuff, and then I looked at the ingredients: one of the first things on it was corn-a grain known to cause allergies in dogs. As a matter of fact, corn might be the most allergy-prone food amongst dogs. Not to mention, thanks to our friends at Monsanto, it is almost always Genetically modified (GMO) or a franken-food, unless it's organic. Real "premium" dog foods brag that they've no corn.Right at the top of the list of ingredients is chicken-by-products, another no-no among real "premium" dog foods. This means all the waste that it is usually thrown out from the chicken since it's not real meat. Cheap dog foods put this in their food simply because it's cheap, but it's not real meat. A little further down you will notice BHA, an artificial preservative, which again real "premium" dog foods do not have.In short, BilJac is a joke. It is basically, insanely overpriced Alpo, with no organic ingredients whatsoever, and uses all the shortcuts, filler, and chemistry that the cheapest dog foods on the market use. Don't be fooled by the old-fashioned appeal of the company's 60 years in business. There is nothing but modern chemistry in the bag, and all the folksy appeal will not make up for cheap, lousy ingredients. I ended up buying Blue Buffalo Wilderness and I have not looked back.
Label: negative

Text: It is second nature for me to check ingredients before purchasing a product.  I am pleased overall with the ingredient listing of this product and really like the taste.  I am not sure where they get their yeast extract because this term can be a "hidden" form of MSG, but have not had a problem using this product.  The corn ingredient is organic, so I am willing to think it not GMO.  I was very glad to see they use sea salt. The fact they use sea salt was one of the reasons I bought this product. Rapunzel products are very tasty and, in my opinion, one of the best for making a good soup or broth.Many people avoid salt thinking it is a good thing. The harmful "salt" to avoid is the product we grew up with, you know, the "salts" in boxes off the grocery store shelf. The body doesn't recognize or break down the heated, hardened crystals of these unnatural, refined so-called salts. The salts found in nature will quickly dissolve in liquids and are recognized and used by the body.  Sea salts, Himalayan salts, etc. also contain minerals and are beneficial and I have used these "real" salts with no problem at all for many years.  Do some research on the internet and discover the healing properties of natural salts.
Label: positive

Text: The standard curry Paste by Kitchens of India (KoI) makes for a quick and easy method to making a rich and flavorful curry.  To make the curry it is a simple process that only takes me no more than 30 minutes to make.  Throw about 1.0 lbs of chicken in a pan (you could use fish or vegetables if you want).  Add the entire packet of paste + 1 cup of water.  Just mix until the paste blends with the water and dissolves and well there you go!  Just simmer for about 20-25 minutes before turning the heat up to high for about 3-5 minutes and you are done.  A delicious curry with chicken(or vegetable/fish) in under 30 minutes.The curry itself tastes best when hot, in my opinion.  There is a mild spice to this curry and while it is a good quality product, this curry also tends to taste a little on the salty side.  It is also a bit sweeter than the other chicken curry that KoI sells (Butter Chicken Curry).  While I do enjoy this curry it is not my favorite.  That goes to Kitchens of India Paste for Butter Chicken Curry which has a bit more spice to it.OVERALL this is a good curry that doesn't have too big of a bite in terms of spice.  It has rich flavor and can go best with chicken, though vegetables and fish also work well for this curry.  Being easy to make in under 30 minutes, this is a good mild curry that will not disappoint.* Nutritional Information ** (Entire packet)Each packet is 3.5oz (100g) and contains FOUR Servings. Entire Packet values listed below, individual serving in []Calories: 180 [45]Total Fat: 12g [3g] / Saturated Fat: 0g / Trans Fat: 0gCholesterol: 0mgSodium: 2200mg [550mg]Total Carbs: 16g [4g]Fiber: 8g [2g] / Sugar: 8g [2g]Protein: 4g [1g]Calcium: 16% [4%] / Iron: 24% [6%]
Label: positive

Text: I started trying this line of curry pastes when I moved into a neighborhood that had lots of Indian and Pakistani restaurants. I came to love the food and wanted to make some at home, but didn't know how to make my own curry. Seems complicated and time-consuming. Luckily, the supermarkets near me carry foods that appeal to the same folks, and that's how I discovered this brand.I've enjoyed their curry pastes very much. It's so easy, the ingredients are healthy, and the best part is, it tastes like someone's Mama made it at home (and this Mama knows what she's doing). I've enjoyed their Hyderabadi and Lamb pastes, too, but this is my favorite because it's milder, more comfortable for a late night dinner with the Mrs., and goes well with lots of ingredients. Stir it in a deep frying pan or a medium saucepan, with a cup of water, and toss in the veggies or meat you think you'd like. Bring to a boil, then simmer till the meat's cooked or the veggies are as tender as you like. It can be ready in 10-12 minutes if you're just doing soft veggies like zucchini and mushroom.Tonight I made it with chicken breast tenderloins, peas, zucchini, a little carrot, half a cup of mushrooms, most of a Cali avocado, 1 tomato, 4-5 scallions. I simmered it 18 minutes and it's so, so tasty, rich and delicious.Really great that this lush, inviting food is totally free of preservatives, too! No cholesterol, low fat, low sugar, and packed with exotic flavor. I LOVE IT.
Label: positive

Text: These oils are great to use instead of zest or extract.  They are packed with flavor and taste fresh, not fake or alcoholic like many extracts tend to do.  I bought a glass eye dropper to dispense the oil, do not use one with a plastic tube as it can damage it and leave the plastic scarred.  Keep this in mind too if you are using the oils to flavor seltzer water, use glass and not a plastic tumbler.  It tastes fresher and more full bodied than any store-bought flavored seltzer and you only need one drop for a 24 oz glass.I use the lime in Thai dishes, the lemon in chicken marinades and a drop of the orange in cookies is very tasty -- there are a million uses for them.  You can even use them for cocktails.Be aware that once opened, you must keep them in the refrigerator.The gift pack contains an ounce of each oil in a cute little bottle with a narrow neck.  The amazon price breaks down to less than $3 a bottle which I think is a great value.  Since I only need a few drops per recipe, they last a long time.  It is also nice to have the smaller bottles so you have fresher oil; a 1 ounce bottle in the fridge is fresher at the end of its use than a 3 ounce bottle sitting in the fridge for three times as long.  I keep the extras in my pantry until they are needed.
Label: positive

Text: This is the larger size of the Sprout Master sprouter, great for larger batches of leafy sprouts or bean sprouts. The optional tray divider makes it easy to make larger batches or separate smaller sprout batches. Half of this tray still has more capacity than the whole of one of the Sprout Master small trays. If you're growing for one or two people I would recommend the "Sprout Master Mini Triple" instead. Coming with three trays the mini triple would allow you to grow a more diverse or more staggered crop.The two "lids" that come with this tray are exactly the same. Put one below to catch any water and use the one on top if you want to block off light (this helps prevent the growth of green leaves, which is usually how bean sprouts are grown). The Sprout Master's drainage holes allow excess water to drain off very effectively.If you've sprouted in a jar before, you'll probably find this to be much easier. Hold the tray under running cool water two or three times a day, then allow excess water to run off before putting the tray away. The sprouts should be thoroughly rinsed to prevent spoilage, kept moist at all times, but not allowed to sit in water. (By comparison, with jar sprouting you'd need to fill and drain the jar of sprouts repeatedly to rinse the sprouts well, then it's more difficult to get them properly drained.)One minor complaint is that some of the smallest seeds can get caught in the drain holes. In my experience, broccoli is large enough to be safe, but with alfalfa or clover you might get a few seeds stuck. When you're cleaning the tray after use you may need to get a small poking instrument to knock one or two out again. The "Easy Sprouter" device doesn't have this problem, but allows slightly less light to reach your sprouts because of it's shape. Neither is a very significant complaint, and I find that the Easy Sprouter gets sufficient light for most purposes.
Label: positive

Text: This is my first sprouter, so I have no basis for comparison.  I looked at a lot of products when I was purchasing this one, and I feel that it's the best tray sprouter on the market for the price.Pros:-Easy to use-Allows for nice aeration-Tray system works well as bottom or lid (I switch top & bottom after every rinse to minimize bacteria & mold growth)-Great size, perfect crop size for two people.  If I get really in to sprouting I may purchase another large size and have the crops rotating.-Holes are small enough that very few seeds get stuckCons:-Small seeds can get stuck in holes-Sprouts will grow through holes and can be hard to pull out without breaking the roots-Not dishwasher safe, and needs to be bleached each time-Not the sturdiest plastic, so I doubt it will last more than 2 years without crackingI have only had one failure so far, buckwheat groats.  They rotted, and I suspect that I did not rinse them voraciously enough.  All other crops (salad mixes with primarily alfalfa & radish, some lentils) have been absolutely perfect with very little maintenance.
Label: positive

Text: Before I bought this sprouter, I used a jar with a screen lid on it and had failure after failure. I don't know what I was doing wrong, but it seemed like I never could get those alfalfa sprouts to "sprout maturity"! Since I got this baby, I am the green queen of sprouting! (at least I feel like it...)I was a little concerned about the plastic stuff, but it seems very stable and does not impart any plastic "flavor" to the greens. I am so pumped that I am moving on to other sproutables. It is very easy to use, and I rinse once in the morning and once before I go to bed. That seems to be enough, but you can rinse more often. I have good water (no chlorine or fluoride) and with this tray my sprouts grow, grow, grow!When I'm ready to harvest them, the "mat" they form is easy to sort of peel up. I put them in a large salad spinner and fill it with water and sort of loosen them up, freeing the hulls to float on the top. Then I get a small strainer (everyone has one of those, right?) and put it over the kitchen sink drain and pour out the water into the strainer. That's where the hulls go (then into the compost bin) instead of down the drain! Then, I whir the salad spinner and get the sprouts "dry" and in they go to a container in the fridge with the lid cracked--so they can "breathe" a little bit.I have been doing this since I started growing with this sprouter. I understand you can actually stack these to grow more, but I will have to wait a bit before I become that adventurous.Oh, yeah, one more thing....After you soak the seeds for 6 or more hours (directions come with the sprouter or you can go to their website) and have them in the sprouter, I adjust the lid so it's open about an inch. Gives them room to breathe.Get it! Your body will thank you for growing LIVE food for it! Be good to yourself and discover sprouting---so much CHEAPER than the stores!!
Label: positive

Text: I first tried sprouting with a jar with a drain lid. It was hard to drain the water all the way out, it was a pain to rinse the seeds, and my sprouts would rot, before they even sprouted. Air couldn't get to them, and I live in Nebraska, a very arid region.Then I got this thing. If you can't grow sprouts in this, just rinse them more. It drains effortlessly. The instructions are ridiculously easy to follow. You can grow tons of sprouts really fast. I grew, tall, strong, deep green, and super potent sprouts in a matter of days. They will not go bad if you keep them in here and keep rinsing them, I rinse about 3 times a day, but you could probably get away with once depending on where you live. You will basically have a living, growing food, that you can eat at your leisure.The smallest seed I have put in them so far is radish, but I'm sure smaller seeds could work, and you could always put a growing sponge on the bottom.During the winter I will get a second one because it will be one of the only ways to get fresh greens in my area, and I'm a vegetarian, so that is super important to me.These are made in the US.
Label: positive

Text: Sorry, but I only like to eat the tops of the sprouts. I only grow broccoli sprouts at the moment. With this sprouter, the roots grow down through the holes and the sprouts grow up vertically. To prevent the roots from matting under the tray, I raise the tray up by putting a flat rock (from my yard!) in a foil baking pan. (appx 11x13 from the dollar store). I fill the pan 3/4 with water, and set the tray on the rock. The roots go down to the water and drink it up. I change the water and rinse the sprouts daily. When ready, I pull the sprouts out the top of the tray, cut off most of the dangling roots, and I'm all set. Easy to clean under running water with dish cloth. I also soak the tray in vinegar water before re sprouting. No, seeds do not get stuck in the holes. Sometimes a few roots get stuck, but they are easily dislodged with the dish cloth rubbed over the holes, front and back. I always thought sprouts needed sun, and I was using a grow light over them, especially in the winter. The directions said they can grow in the dark! Did I misunderstand? I decided to turn off the grow light and just place them on a table by the window that gets a little light in the afternoon, and they grow just fine. I'm now ordering the set of three trays for a better value, and I'll try other seeds. I may have to eat the roots on the larger seeds, but I can manage that!
Label: positive

Text: These add a creamy,dynamic cinnamon/vanilla flavor to almost anything but once in a while I experience a very strange aftertaste with the chocolate flavor (just the chocolate: I've tried the choc, cinn/van and Irish Cream),usually when I use 2 vs just one in my beverage.The packaging, however, leaves a lot to be desired and unless you want a lot of work when this arrives, you might want to rethink this purchase. In recent weeks, I've purchased 3 cases of this product (that is 12 boxes of 50 single serve cups) in 3 flavors for the local emergency shelter here in Western, MA due to the tornadoes that ripped through my community and those of my family and friends. I am so thankful I looked at this product before just bringing it to the make-shift kitchens and coffee setups on the back of pickup trucks. This is what I experienced. What follows is actually my 'cut and paste' comments from one of my 'leave packaging comments' for Amazon, so it lists 2 cases vs 3. I purchased 1 first, then had second thoughts and went back and bought the additional 2 flavors (creamy chocolate, cinnamon vanilla (excellent flavor, by the way) and Irish Cream (a bit too subtle in flavor for me-could have a bit more oomph). As such, you'll see me refer to just 2 below.Exterior shipping box was fine and interior 'case' holding product perfectly intact. However, contents (50 in each)are a sticky, gooey, unusable mess. I purchased 2 flavors/2 cases = 8 cartons = 400 of this product and all 8 cartons are a disgusting disappointment. While the majority of the single serve cups are fine, a large handful in every one of the 50-pack boxes are squished, cracked or just ripped open. Contents spilled over the rest of the single serve cups throughout the boxes that are now slimy, sticky and tacky, with what looks like dried and/or tacky curdled milk all over them. I had to throw away 46 cups total and wash every other single serve cup by hand and then find another container to hold them all. I sure won't be buying these again.
Label: positive

Text: When looking at buying creamer, I mistakenly believed that this product would pair well with the coffee I enjoy (a cinnamon / vanilla flavored coffee).  Listed below are my opinions about said creamer when used in both normal coffee (unflavored medium roast) as well as flavored coffee.  Both types of coffee were ground that day.The good:The amount of creamer you receive borders on the ridiculous.  The package has to contain near 8 lbs. of creamer.The creamer is individually packaged and arrives in four cartons (each containing 50 creamers).The cartons are easy to open and have a flap to allow for easy access.The bad:The creamer is VERY weak.  To receive the same texture / coloring / flavoring as a traditional creamer (one in a bottle), you must use roughly 50% more of this creamer.The vanilla taste is very minimal.The cinnamon taste is very prevalent.the cinnamon taste is rather repugnant and almost has a sour taste to it.The individual creamers are rather hard to open (not that they are difficult to grab the flap, but there is a ton of glue used, and most often (7/10 times) will tear because the flap is glued down to heavily.~~~~In all, I would not recommend this product.  I've actually tried to give the creamer away to several people, but after tasting it, no one wants it.  Even at work, with the ~175 remaining creamers sitting there, after a week, the first container is still not empty (meaning, that of the 175 I brought, there are still more than 150 sitting there).
Label: negative

Text: Although I've only been able to try three of Caribou's k cup blends (Mahogany as well as Caribou Coffee, Caribou Blend, K-Cup Portion Pack for Keurig K-Cup Brewers, 24-Count and Caribou Coffee, Daybreak Morning Blend, K-Cup Portion Pack for Keurig K-Cup Brewers, 24-Count), I've found that Caribou consistently produces the highest quality k cups. They are head and shoulders above their competitors like Green Mountain and Van Houtte.I should mention that Caribou's Daybreak blend is far and away my favorite k cup flavor of the 20 or so I've been able to sample so far. But I enjoy a flavorful medium roast. My boyfriend, on the other hand, prefers an extra bold dark roast. After our favorable experience with the Caribou brand before, we picked up a box of this coffee and hoped that it would be a great choice for a dark blend.Let me just say that Mahogany is full of smokey delicious flavor. It's quite strong and dark, but there is no rancid bitterness or acidity that accompanies a lot of the dark roasts. This coffee packs a powerful punch. Although that isn't normally my thing, I can enjoy a cup of this with milk and a bit of sweetener. My boyfriend adores it, though, and it has become a staple blend for him. I will still drink this on occasion, but it's a bit too potent for me.I think this is the best dark roast I've had the chance to try.
Label: positive

Text: This is a medium roasted coffee, those that call it a light roast must be used to a heavier tasting cup of coffee. When first brewed, it has a very pleasant nutty aroma that fills the room and gets your expectations going. I have the Keurig with 3 cup sizes, if you brew it on the smallest setting, it's like a little shot of nutty espresso (on the milder side)...on the second setting it's a more intense flavor that Starbucks people could probably relate to...on the largest setting it is ready for you to add what you desire and still get a very good, subtle nutty tasting, rich coffee. I add a bit of sugar while drinking it black and it's a delight...when you are in the mood for something different. I find I don't like it as much as my first cup of the day...but every once in a while I like a cup in the afternoon and this is where I find it stands out. Not a heavy, burnt tasting roast...milder than that heavy stuff and flavorful WITHOUT knocking you out with an added fake flavor that doesn't belong there...it seems to really go together, pecan & coffee. Let's just say it's a nice cup of coffee with a bit of a nutty tone...PS: I'm not a real lover of flavored coffees but this one is done right...on the lines of Island Coconut, who would think that coconut & coffee flavors would go pleasantly together?...but they do!
Label: positive

Text: i have been using fouresent bulbs in my house for a long time and the reson for this is that the wattage of the bulbs doesn't brake me up in electrical cost but when i ordered the earthmate bulb i didn't know what full spetrum was untill i put one of these bulbs in and saw the light output that i have been looking for since i been buying fouresent bulbs and the light is a light blue and now i can see things better because it a lot brighter that the other one i have been using so i am staying with these bulbs for her on out,fouresent bulbs save you a lot of money per year when you calculate out from a incondesent 60w light bulb to a 13w or 15w fouresent bulb i can use 4 15w flouresent bulbs compared to 1 60w incandesent light bulb because 60w bulb is 1/2 amp of electricity on your electrical circuit and after you start using this type of electricity in a years time starts to add up into money and i don't have that type of money to pay someone salery in the power companys, so i stay with fouresent bulbs only...

Text: I was very hopeful about this pie filling, since I love rhubarb and can't get it since moving to the South (not unless I pay through the nose).  However, I've only opened one of the two jars I bought, so far, and that jar WAS a generous amount for filling an 8 or 9-inch pie, but had what I consider to be much too thick a consistency (possibly as a result of all the sugar and other sweeteners necessary for the preserving process? I don't know).In addition, I may've simply gotten one 'bad' jar, but the flavor seemed 'off' -- almost on the edge of fermentation.  I 'cut' the jar of pie filling with a pound of fresh strawberries and turned it into a decent-ish 10-inch strawberry-rhubarb pie, but it still doesn't taste quite right to me, and there's still that slight hint of fermentation.  I can eat it, but I'm not in any hurry to finish it off.Perhaps my expectations were unreasonable, since I've never had a canned rhubarb pie filling before (not even home-canned), but always used fresh or home-frozen rhubarb, going easy on the sugar in the recipe to preserve that mouth-watering tartness.  Whenever I've had access to lots of rhubarb, I prefer it straight, no strawberries or blueberries or raspberries mixed in (though those are all good combinations).Honestly, I'm beginning to think it might be worthwhile to pay over $4 a pound for a bit of fresh rhubarb in the store, which I can cut with the much-cheaper strawberries to make a semi-rhubarb pie.  But that's just my taste, and as I said, I might've simply gotten a 'bad batch'.
Label: neutral

Text: Okay, it's kashi, it's healthy, it's full of real food, in as much, these are more substantial (read thick and grainy) than a regular cookie...these are not going to taste like standard oatmeal raisin cookies...the cookies are rather dry (and i do have some concerns about the little packs in the boxes that keep the cookies ...umm fresh) I'm not sure how safe/healthy those are but i'm not a scientist so i'll leave it at that (I guess they are moisture control) anyhow, all in all these cookies are good for someone who want's a cookie but without the guilt factor. These are one at a time sized cookies, and I actually can't imagine wanting to eat more than one at a sitting anyhow because they are kind of hard to work through (See grainy/dry) but the flavor overall is good, I prefer this better than the dark chocolate or trail mix, I think the raisins add just a bit of moisture so this variety is a touch less dry, and also a tiny bit sweeter. Also, something to note, if you are buying kashi items in bulk be prepared to eat a lot of them and quickly as the expiration dates are usually no more than 2 or 3 months past purchse (afterall these don't have all the chemicals/preservatives of your typical quick foods. That being said, all in all, this is my favorite of the three kashi cookie varieties,and they are a healthy option for cookie lovers.
Label: positive

Text: I tried some of this on my old guy, an American Staffordshire Terrier that is 14 years, 8 months old. He had just gone through a phase of refusing to eat his normal food, which is good stuff, but is a dry kibble mini disc type ( Bench & Field Holistic - Very good food! )So I got some canned foods to try on him.  Got the Newman's, and 2 other organic kinds.  Buster just loves the canned foods, but the consistency of some of the others is sloppier and harder for him to eat, as they just seem to mash into the bowl, and he has to lick them instead of being able to eat them in soft chunks.  This food smells almost good enough to make me hungry...... and is easy to spoon into his dish, where it takes him less than a nano-second to select a nice chunk and eat it.  I just stand there, and spoon a few chunks at a time into his bowl and he looks for more.So I am really happy with this food and even if he DOES get spoiled on it, he's so worth spoiling at his advanced age that I don't consider it a problem!Before this, I was cooking human food to tempt him just to eat, so this is better balanced and seems to give him more energy.( Before this, he was feed on free schedule, with a bowl of dry food out at all times, and he would eat every 3 days when he felt like it. That worked very well for 14 plus years!  I did the same with my first AmStaff, and he made it to 15. ) Also, Buster has NEVER had to make a vet visit for illness or medical problem his entire life until 2 weeks ago, so that eating arrangement obviously worked great for a very long time!  ; )
Label: positive

Text: All you need with this is a sprinkle...if that.  It does sweeten everything - and is good if you have diabetes or just want to avoid sugar.  Too much and it turns bitter tasting on ya, so not too much.  Some people think there is an after taste...or an off taste - but I don't.  No, its not going to taste like a mouthful of real sugar...but it does add sweetness / like you know, diet coke is sweet but it not coke.  The powder can be messy, the drops are easier - but both cumbersome to carry around with you (big disadvantage).  The little packets of this (and they make them) contain maltodextrin - which I avoid...a filler with a high glycemic index (basically corn starch) so know that in advance.  And the packets some of them contain INULIN which is a fiber - and that can cause painful gas.  This is all I know - I hope its been helpful.  Should you try it?  Yeah.  Don't toss it after your first go'round with it though - give it another try and another until you learn how and when/where it will work best for you.  If you want to cook with this please learn about that on the web - I have no idea about measurement conversions for this.  Enjoy.
Label: positive

Text: I've been experimenting with homemade sugar-free chocolate for a while now, and after trying a large number of sugar substitutes, I've found it very difficult to make anything that I'm proud of.  Chocolate, in general, is very temperamental.  It has six different crystalline forms, and you have to get the temperature just right or else you wind up with a less than desirable form.  And your sugar substitute needs to be fat-soluble and anhydrous, because small amounts of water can will cause your chocolate to seize.  It's very complicated, and I've never been keen on anything I've made myself.Coco Polo's chocolate, by contrast, is tempered perfectly.  It has just the right snap and mouth-feel.  Because it's flavored with Stevia and Erythritol (the easiest-on-your-stomach sugar alcohol), you can eat the whole bar in one sitting and not suffer any unpleasant effects.  At least I didn't.  Everyone is different.  But I can say that even small amounts of malitol-sweetened chocolate cause me gastric upset.That being said, this product is not as sweet as ordinary sugared chocolate.  But that is going to be true of any sugar-free chocolate.  In particular, Coco Polo's sweetness is also somehow more subtle, which is the best I can explain it.  If your familiar with Stevia as a sweetener in tea and coffee, you'll know what I'm talking about.  It's not as intense as sugar, but it's still nice and doesn't have any chemical aftertaste.  If you are a fan of less sweet dark-style chocolates, than transitioning to this sugar free band is easy.If I didn't know any better I would give this product 3 or 4 stars because it is not as good as regular chocolate.  But having tried to make my own for years, I recognize when I've been outdone and I'm giving it a 5 star review.  I've also tried many brands of sugar free chocolate over the years, and I like this one the best.On the downside, I think this chocolate is too pricey.  When I ordered mine, it was shipped in an insulated container with cold packs to keep it stable.  This was in the spring and early fall - perhaps in the winter they forgo this treatment.  (They don't ship at all in the heat of summer.)  So perhaps the extra cost is to cover the special shipping.
Label: positive

Text: My daughter was on Good Start Gentle before finally moving to Similac Alimentum when her milk protein allergy was diagnosed. After 6 months of spending $500+ on formula, we were given the go ahead to try Good Start again. She struggled with the taste (Alimentum tastes AWFUL and Gerber Good Start is sweet in comparison) at first, but we tried switching her again by giving Good Start as the first bottle of the day and she had no problems. She's been on it for 6 weeks now and has no more gas or fussiness than she did on Alimentum. We love Gerber Good Start!When she was on Good Start Gentle previously, we used the 8.45 oz cans or the quart-size cans. Neither of those can be found anywhere. The new packing is great and weighs a lot less. I buy it as Babies R Us or Target - they always seem to have some in stock, even if it's only the 4-packs ($6.29 at Target!). The 24 pack cases seem to run about $39.99 when they are in stock. Babies R Us has had two specials in a row on them (buy 2, get $10 off instantly; then buy 2, get $15 off instantly), which they honor on multiple purchases in one visit. They also do rainchecks if it's not in stock.Hopefully Amazon will get some more in stock soon - we go through 4 cases a month!
Label: positive

Text: I was alarmed when I suddenly became unable to find this product in any local grocery store. I visited Amazon and was relieved to find it available here, but was troubled by the multiple reviews complaining that the packaging was poor and that the jars arrived broken. So I wrote to Amazon Customer Service to inquire about the situation. I received a prompt, courteous reply saying that the packaging problem had been corrected and that I could order with confidence. So I did. The six jars arrived the other day, IMPECCABLY packaged--each jar individually wrapped in bubble wrap, the six jars carefully packed in a box with additional cushioning, that box packed in a second box with cushioning. All six jars were in perfect condition.As for the product, I have relied on Nestea Unsweetened 100% Instant for years. It is the only instant iced tea I have ever been able to find that is absolutely pure, without any sweeteners, flavorings, or additives. That may not be everyone's cup of tea (so to speak), but if you like your iced tea straight, or if you prefer to add your own sweetener or lemon, this is the product to buy. (BTW, when I'm feeling like a bit of sweetness and tartness in my iced tea, I add a spoonful of all-natural frozen limeade concentrate. It's terrific.)
Label: positive

Text: This product has become UNAVAILABLE in local stores here in southeastern PA.  What has happened is that America has a love affair with sugar.  So much so, that if the food they put in their mouth is not loaded with it, they don't think it tastes good.  23g of sugar is a federal requirement in 12 oz. soft drinks, or at least it seems so.  As I've gotten older, I've given up things that obviously contain lots of sugar.  But companies are sneaky.  They know that sugar works and is addictive.  Sugar is cheaper than tea.  Add more sugar, remove some tea.  The formula is a winner.  That explains the skyrocketing diabetes in the U.S., but the companies don't care.  And it's not their fault.  Nobody forces you to take these items off the shelf and put them in your cart.  Until maybe now.  I used this concentrated tea to make my own iced tea for years.  Maybe it's just my fellow citizens that don't buy it anymore, but vendors have dropped it from the shelves.  All my visitors to my home always commented that my iced tea was very good.  I'd use the standard sugared mix, but cut off the last 1/3 of the recommended dosage, and instead added this concentrate to make it more tea, less sugar.  It works, and it works well.  But now here I am on Amazon, buying a product that I should be able to find in stores, but no dice.  And no, I don't want to spend $10.00 on energy charges to boil a boatload if teabags and deal with all of that.  This is easy.  Kudos to the seller: fast service, reasonable price.  Good health to you and your family. For the rest of you out there, don't forget to brush.
Label: positive

Text: Hey Gang,My tea arrived in a box within a box with some bubble wrap and packaging.  However, I found the box in the middle of my driveway on a hot day (which is a bad omen to begin with).  Why wasn't it on the porch?  Why not at least leaning up against the garage?  Was it thrown onto the driveway by a harried UPS worker?  What I do know is that I can't use one of the jars (it arrived broken) and it's not returnable.  In today's economy, I'm trying to make smarter decisions with where I throw my money.  This tea product is a luxury for me.  I, like you, have roamed around stores without luck of finding the product.  I decided I'd go with Plan B which is to search the Internet and was thrilled to find it on Amazon.  However, Plan C is to "move on" and most likely start making "Cold Brewed Iced Tea" from tea bags.  Good luck if you order this... but maybe it's time to "move on."  Lipton is thriving on the shelves but Nestea tastes better in my opinion! I give the tea 4 stars for taste but one star for packaging.
Label: negative

Text: I first purchased this in a package Crystal Light Mocktails Drink Mixes, 3-Flavors, 15-Count along with the Appletini and Margarita flavors. The Appletini and Margarita were good, but not as good as this Mojito Drink Mix.  This has the taste of lime and is sweet but also a little tart, much like a real Mojito.  It also has a slight minty taste which I really like. It doesn't have aftertaste.Each canister contains 5 packets that will make 2 quarts each.  I usually use them in my Contigo AUTOSPOUT® Addison 24 oz. Water Bottles 3 pack Blue, Charcoal, Honeysuckle and just mix it to taste. It has 5 calories per serving which is 1/8 of a packet so it's good when you're trying to watch the calories.  The ingredients are: Citric Acid, Maltodextrin, Aspartame (Sweetener), Acesulfame Potassium (Sweetener), Sugar, Magnesium Oxide, Contains Less Than 2% of:, Natural Flavor, Yellow 5, Blue 1I like it best over ice.  I fill my water bottle about 3/4 of the way full add the Mojito Drink Mix and then fill the bottle the rest of the way with ice. It dissolves very easily. It also makes a good cocktail in a pinch if you add rum or vodka. Two thumbs up for this great product from Kraft Foods.
Label: positive

Text: These bulbs make my room (painted blue) look absolutely vivid and beautiful, specially compared to my previous 27K CFL lightbulbs.I have ALZO 55K lightbulbs, love it so decided to change all my 27K CFL lightbulbs to 55K in my first floor. Amazon sent me the package, the first shipment was wrong items, 27K bulbs. I returned and asked for replacement. Amazon shipped me the right one but with two broken light bulbs. I bought all from Amazon unless it does not carry.Amazon shipped me the replacement with no charge. Text: Since my husband and I are summer person who live in 4 season place, I bought these light bulbs to make our cold winter little more cheerful. These bulbs come with eco-packaging which I like, and it was bonus that I didn't have to deal with bulky packaging. First I turned on the light, I noticed it is little dim, but after couple minutes, it reaches full strength. For my small bedroom (10'X 7'), one light bulb was enough to make the room bright.When I read while I was lying down, I noticed that the light actually doesn't hurt my eyes even if I look directly. I do have very sensitive eyes, so I was very happy that this bulbs don't make my eyes tired.Also, I bought more lamps and had short of these bulbs, so I went ahead and used my plain old light bulb. My husband and I could not believe that how dim, irritating the yellow light bulb was compared to our full spectrum bright soft white light bulbs. I don't think I can go back to normal light bulb ever again, and now I told all my families to change theirs as well. They are all happy just like me. Energy saving is extra bonus on top of every benefit to have this light bulb.

Text: When I ordered these, based on the description, I thought the honey was somehow hardened into stick-form and thus would 'melt' into a cup of hot tea.  I was surprised when I discovered these sticks were actually thin plastic straws filled with honey!  There is no way I'm going to insert a plastic straw into a hot cup of tea. I'm not sure it would work anyway; I suppose you could cut open each end and then blow the honey in the tea.  Does Stash think you're going to sip on these straws?  I don't know what the company was thinking when they produced this product, other than as a candy to be eaten.I believe the term "stick" does not accurately describe this product and is misleading.  I doubt it's intentional, however.  The Stash Tea Company is an excellent company and has many high quality teas and products.The honey flavored with chai spices is good enough but frankly, it it a very expensive way to buy honey.  Better to buy a jar and flavor it yourself.  I wound up using these Chai Honey Sticks by cutting open one end and pressing out the honey into a tea cup/pot, which was messy and time consuming.I love Stash tea... drink it every morning.  But this product is a bust and I would never purchase it again.
Label: negative

Text: I have three power-chewers, and pressed rawhide bones are a must-have around our house. My dogs will quickly destroy cheap rawhides. With the choking hazard presented by bones that come apart too easily, it can be a challenge to find a decent pressed rawhide without breaking the bank. We were paying $5 - $7 a piece for a good quality bone at our favorite pet store. We can go through as many as 5 a week...ouch. Adds up fast.I dug around to try to find a quality replacement that would save us some money and stumbled on these on Amazon. I figured they would be flimsy for the price, but decided to give it a shot. I was astonished at the quality. They are great. These bones last as long as the ones I was buying from the pet store. The price is unbeatable. About a $1.70 a piece. That's a HUGE price difference. Even the box is great. We tear off the front flap, the way the item is pictured, and put the whole thing in a deep drawer in our kitchen. Very handy for us. We average about 1 box every three months. I haven't really counted, but it seems we burn through less of these than the others, too.I am a firm believer that what you don't pay out in quality food and treats for your dogs, you pay out in vet bills, poorer health and shorter life span. So we buy top quality pet foods, etc. I really love it when quality comes with a great price, too. These have made three dogs VERY happy. They've made my wallet VERY happy, too. Excellent bang for your buck! I highly recommend these.
Label: positive

Text: For this review you're going to get two opinions.  Darshan1 and chocolate enthusiast extraordinaire, Darshan's Mom.Darshan says:  As an amateur chocolate consumer, I found this fairly similar in taste to Hershey's Special Dark at first, um, chomp, but after a couple chews, I noticed it's a fair amount creamier and much more satisfying than Special Dark.  I favor milk chocolate (I think Cadbury milk is my favorite) so this was a little bitter for me (it actually is a little stronger than Special Dark), but compared to some 82% stuff that my mom sometimes has where I feel like I'm going to choke for the bitterness, this was quite good.  A very good compromise (in my opinion) if we were going to split a chocolate bar for dessert. I like the fact that it's "Fair Trade" chocolate since a lot of cocoa harvesting involves some harsh child labor practices, and since it's one of the few fair trade brands that I can get at the nearby regular grocery store instead of having to trek to a gourmet store, I'd probably start buying their milk chocolate variety.Darshan's Mom says:  Newman's Own organic dark chocolate satisfyingly combines the creaminess of milk chocolate with the taste of a somewhat mild, but luscious, dark chocolate.  Since it's merely 54% chocolate, the heavenly, bitter tang of 75% to 8o% chocolate is missing.  However, the rich smoothness of the product almost makes up for that.  Being a confirmed chocoholic, I've taste tested chocolate in France, Belgium and Switzerland, the holy trinity of European chocolate according to a local chocolate store owner, but, in order to be perfectly fair, of course, I've forced myself to sample chocolate in Germany and Italy as well. I've made pilgrimages to American restaurants famous for their chocolate temptations, visited a cocoa plantation in Tobago and prepared numerous chocolate desserts over the years.  So, I think I know chocolate.  Yes, for those of us who crave deep, dark chocolate, Newman's Own comes up a little short, but it is surprisingly delicious nevertheless.  Another plus, it's organic.  If it's priced well compared to the better-known European chocolate bar brands, it could easily become one of my top choices for everyday enjoyment.
Label: positive

Text: Perhaps they should have given this bar a different name, maybe "Dark Chocolate for Beginners". It is a "Dark Chocolate" bar but with only 54% cacao. It is still more than typical milk chocolate (in the 30s), but it may confuse fans of dark chocolate. This is more of a bridging-the-gap type of a chocolate. For someone coming to this from the off-the-shelf mass-market milk chocolate, this is not as "bitter" as typical dark chocolate, so if you want to eat healthier chocolate, this may be an option. It is also sweet, sweeter than most of the dark chocolates (60%) I recall trying out, and it does not have the "bitter" taste.However, for someone coming to this from the 60% and 70% and 80% dark chocolate, this may come as a surprise, as it is sweeter and it doesn't have the high percentage of cocoa one would expect. Newman also has a 70% "Super Dark" chocolate Newman's Own Organics Super Dark (70%) Chocolate Bar, 3.25-Ounce Bars (Pack of 12) but I haven't tried that so I can't really compare. However, the choice of a name (70% being called Super Dark) is a hint that these are perhaps intended more for the mass-market grocery store consumer, not the dark chocolate connoisseur.Nutrition-wise, the whole chocolate bar (2.25oz or 64 grams) has 330 calories and it provides one-third of the RDA fat calories and two-thirds of the RDA saturated fat calories. There is 0% cholesterol, 0% trans-fat and only traces of sodium (5mg). Also good is 4g of fiber and 3g of protein, but a jumping up and down 28g of sugars. It is candy after all :-) The cocoa is Rainforest Alliance certified.The bar is made in a way that can be broken in six (probably uneven unless you are careful) pieces, so theoretically you can break it into six servings, which significantly lowers the calories and fat intake. More realistically, one-third of the bar is a reasonable compromise for one person per day, bringing it down to 110 calories with 10% RDA fat and 22% RDA saturated fat. (the numbers are based on the standard 2000 calorie diet found in most of the food labels).
Label: positive

Text: I received 1 bar via the Amazon Vine program to review. Let me just say I love chocolate in any form, but dark chocolate is probably 4th down on the list of favorites.  That being said, I find most dark chocolate is either too bitter for my liking, often leaving me with a slight pucker in my mouth and/or a chalky after-taste.  Newman's Own Organics Dark Chocolate was a pleasant surprise on the taste front.  Yes, it's dark chocolate, but no bitter aftertaste.  It's smooth and just sweet enough to remind me of bittersweet chocolate, more than dark.The reason for 4 and not 5 stars has more to do with the price of being environmentally aware.  Perhaps that explains the price hike on this bar of chocolate versus some others?  I love that Newman's company puts a portion of its proceeds back into helping others, and that too may explain the price hike, but I don't have to like it.  Not when other brands are just as tasty and cost much less.Similar chocolate in quality and taste: Dagoba Dark (59%) Semisweet Bar, 2.0-Ounces Bars (Pack of 12) focuses too on being environmentally friendly.  My suggestion if you like dark chocolate and want to help the planet, you consider Dagoba, Newman's Own Organics and Endangered Species Black Panther, Extreme Dark Chocolate (88%), 3-Ounce Bars (Pack of 12) for your own head to head taste test to determine your favorite.If you're going to be purchasing your chocolate with the environment and natural ingredients in mind, you should also figure out which one tastes best to you.
Label: positive

Text: This is great dark chocolate. It is 54% cocoa which might make you think it could be bitter but it is not at all: it has a very light and enjoyable sweet taste. It also has a very silky texture. I have had almost every expensive dark chocolate out there (me a chocolate addict? Big time!) and I have to say this is one of the nicest surprises--a very nice chocolate, not too sweet, still tastes like dark chocolate but none of the "too dark" taste you often get from expensive chocolates. Also, it's organic.You have to hand it to the folks at Newman's Own, they not only find great products, but they do wonderful work in the world. Paul Newman left a legacy that is far larger than his films: he left the world with a dose of great humanity.That said: The Vine newsletter said this would be 12 bars, I got one. When I wrote and asked why, I got a typical runaround from some "customer service" employee (I think they need to rename this people something that better reflects what they really do which is to, as we said in the 60's, shine you on) who gave me the company line. In other words, our mistake, tough luck. But I take this Vine program seriously and put a lot of time and effort into reviewing products and I think if Amazon is going to ask Vine members to review things fairly, they should treat the people who do the reviewing fairly.But the chocolate, out of this world good. And Newman's Own? Perhaps the best organization on earth.
Label: positive

Text: This particular chocolate bar was "perfect" in my opinion, since I often find dark chocolate bars to be "too bitter." Instead, this one seems somewhere in the middle, between the REALLY Dark chocolate and the light chocolate. It is smooth, and enjoyable.While the information lists a bar as a "single serving," I consider one square (there are 6 in a bar) to be adequate to meet my desire for chocolate, and still maintain my girlish figure. ;-)Background for those who care about organics, fair trade, and sustainability:Nell Newman co-founded the Organics line back in 1982, and it split from Newman's Own in 2001. The company has a serious focus on organics, and is a member of the Rainforest Alliance, ensuring that the chocolate is not only grown organically, but also in a socially and ecologically appropriate manner. Newman confirms that all chocolate is purchased at fair trade prices.Since the Rainforest Alliance recently issued a joint statement with Fairtrade and UTZ Certified, you can be assured that this chocolate was not produced by slave labor.For most people considering purchasing these bars, this is not an issue, however, I try to be conscious of social issues, and researched this information for those who find it important.
Label: positive

Text: I pretty much love any kind of chocolate (other than white chocolate which I can't stand). I enjoy milk chocolate as well as dark chocolate so that may explain why I liked this so much. It seems to me that it is in the middle, between the two in flavor. It doesn't have the totally creamy, sweet flavor of milk chocolate, but doesn't have the bite sometimes associated with dark chocolate (and no aftertaste I sometimes find with dark). Some folks may find that being in the middle equals bland but I found it delightful.The bar itself it a bit bigger than an iPhone and bigger than a Hershey's chocolate bar, but it's not huge. The bar was scored into 6 squares and I ate 3 yesterday and 3 today. I found that amount completely satifying and felt no real desire to eat the entire bar in one sitting -- which is a VERY good thing.The organic nature of the bar also appeals to me since I am trying to be more aware of the chemicals I put into my body. I eat my share of junky food, but am trying to eat more fruits and veggies and this organic bar fits in with that.For anyone that enjoys chocolate, this bar is very, very good!
Label: positive

Text: So, I don't really know how to express my feelings over a chocolate bar.  In my household, there is no other choice besides dark chocolate--that's all we do.  Whether it's the conventional Special Dark or the ritzy high end selections, we've tried it all.  I was excited to check out Newman's Own, an organic variation weighing in at 54% cocoa.  When we go upscale, we tend to venture into the 72% range, so I was interested to see the difference.Me:  I liked the bar.  It was appropriately sweet (but perhaps not dark enough) and had a smoothness I enjoyed.  Spouse:  Didn't like it as much as I did.  Not enough cocoa content, rather generic in conception.  Value:  While we split over quality, we agreed that the price tag on this chocolate was probably a bit high.  We've had better alternatives for less--in some cases a lot less!  Obviously, no one is going to hate a chocolate bar--I'd just recommend that you sample one before investing in a large quantity.  Make sure it delivers the bang you require for your buck!3 Stars from my spouse, 4 stars from me--product at 3 1/2 stars.  But since I write the review, I get the round-up!
Label: positive

Text: The Newman's Own Organics Dark Chocolate Bar offers a 2.25 ounces of smooth but unimpressive dark chocolate flavor.  It's of medium taste quality, in my opinion, like it's missing something.Packaged in a small brown and black wrapper, this candy bar is created using 54% cocoa, which has proven to be beneficial for cardiovascular well-being, reducing your risks of blood clots, heart attacks and strokes.  On the downside, this bar contains 330 calories, which compared to a regular candy bar without healthy cocoa ingredients, is a high fat content.  When compared to other bars packed with healthier cocoa, the Newman's Own Organics dark chocolate bar does compare in that regard.Newman's Own gives all of it's profits to charity, and the company motto is "Shameless Exploitation in Pursuit of the Common Good."  They are a company that does the right thing and sets the standard by which other companies should follow.Just for the chocolate, I'd probably pass.  But if I have to have chocolate, and I do have to have it, I'd choose Newman's Own Organics over another brand that doesn't use good business practices and donate their profits to charity; right up there with supporting mom and pop stores and shopping local grocers instead of buying at the nearest big-box super center.So it's not just a chocolate bar.  It's a chocolate bar that pays it forward.
Label: positive

Text: This is a fairly high quality confection.  The flavor is smooth, round, and fruity, almost raisin-y.  However, I am a dark chocolate lover, and dark chocolate lovers won't be seduced by refinement alone.So I consulted my spouse, a light/milk chocolate lover, and we both agree that while tasty, Newman's Own Organics dark lacks that subtle, almost spicy bitterness that brings out the complexity and assertive flavors of chocolate at varying cacao levels.  Now obviously 54% cacao isn't going to register on the palate like 70%, 75% or 90% cacao, but there can still be that "snap" to chocolate, which is often compared to notes in coffee or chili pepper, even at the lower percentages.  I can still, for example, perceive that note in many grocery store brand chocolate morsels, although the overall quality of those will not match the Newman's.  By comparison, the Newman's has a waxiness that reminds me of Dove or Cadbury's.I will say that this is probably a safe choice for company, where a little something sweet is desired that won't be too much for less-than-diehard fans of the dark stuff.  It's agreeable; just not distinctive enough to kill for, which is pretty severe criticism where chocolate is concerned!
Label: negative

Text: Ok, so I am a little biased. I adore the late Paul Newman, his family, his enterprise, his charity, and all of his products. But I also love me some dark chocolate! I take my chocolate tasting very seriously and wouldn't give it high marks just based on sheer good will. This is some seriously good stuff! Even though I love dark chocolate, I am not what one would call a chocoholic. I have restraint. I typically have a bar in my cupboard, then after dinner break off a piece or two. I savor it, then I leave it at that. Tonight I tried a piece of this delicious confection had to eat half the bar all at once. It was SO GOOD! What I love about it is it's creamy consistency. You usually find that in only milk chocolate, whereas dark chocolate usually has a sharp, almost bitter taste to it. You don't get any of that bitter taste in this bar at all - and yet it's a true dark chocolate. How do they do that? There are lots of subtle undertones of flavors, and it makes for an amazing dessert. And of course coming from Paul Newman, it's heaven sent.Bonus too that they took such great care in choosing the best fair-trade organic chocolate. That's a big deal because if you look at most commercial chocolate sources, it makes the diamond industry look tame. This makes Newman's Organic a truly guilt-free delight. Enjoy!
Label: positive

Text: I used to hate dark chocolate. I didn't care for its dryer texture or bitter taste. Then I was diagnosed with gestational diabetes and chocolate pretty much became a no-no. Through careful experimentation though, I discovered I could occasionally enjoy a square of dark chocolate with higher cacao percentages because they have less sugar. I began to appreciate the flavor of chocolate without the milky sweetness and have since been a big fan.Keeping that in mind I'm always trying new brands and blends in search of the perfect dark chocolate bar. Unfortunately, as much as I love Newman's Own as a company and many of their other products this chocolate was just not for me. It's too sweet and the vanilla taste came through strong. It melted into a watery consistency in my mouth and almost had an alcohol-like tang to it. Once I'd noticed that tang I checked the ingredient list and sure enough it's made with chocolate liquor which some may like but I personally don't care for when I reach for dark chocolate.At only 54% cocoa this is pretty low on the spectrum of darker chocolates and with it's texture would be a great introduction to dark chocolate for someone who doesn't like it usually. It kind of reminded me of an oversweetened version of Lindt Lindor Truffles Dark Chocolate, 60-Count Box's centers. If you like organic and fairtrade chocolate this is certainly not a bad place to start but I would recommend trying Theo Classic Organic (70% Cacao) Dark Chocolate, 3-Ounce Bars (Pack of 12) (or any other Theo chocolate bars which are my FAVORITE Organic and Fair trade chocolate products) if you're looking for something similar but with a higher cocoa content.
Label: negative

Text: Newman's is a brand that I'm used to hearing on fig bars, prunes, and other things much healthier than chocolate, so I wasn't sure how good their chocolate was going to be.  I was very pleasantly surprised to find that not only was their chocolate very fancy, but it was also very smooth and not as bitter as other dark chocolates.The packaging, as you can see from the picture on the description page, is very ornately designed, and the bar is the same.  It is divided into sixths, and on each sixth there is an engraved N.The taste, which I was very surprised at, wasn't bitter like Hershey's dark chocolate tends to be, but it wasn't too sweet.  It was just the right bittersweet mixture!  Also, on top of this, it had a very creamy texture, much like milk chocolate would.In one 2.25 ounce bar, there are 330 calories, so I wouldn't necessarily describe this as a healthy snack, but all of the ingredients are organic, making it more nutritious than your average chocolate bar.Overall, it is a deliciously different type of dark chocolate bar that I recommend to all chocoholics (like me).
Label: positive

Text: Note: by "all" I did not mean *my* chocolate.Newman's own organic dark chocolate is really fine chocolate, more like that you would expect to find at a chocolatier than at a grocery store!  There are things about it which are, in my experience, fairly unique.  It is a dark chocolate with the mouth feel and texture approaching milk chocolate.  It is not chalky, bitter or grainy.  There are ways in which the texture approaches truffles. I am guessing that is  because they use cocoa butter instead of some other oil or fat, like most commmercialy available chocolates.  It is 56% dark; not as dark as some, but in my opinion it strikes a nice balance bot6h smooth and dark.It is made from high quality 100% organic and Rainforest Alliance certified; good for you and the planet.  If that's not enough, Newman's own donates 100% of after tax profit to charity, a product you can feel good about no matter how you look at it!I would say without reservation that htis is the best organic dark chocolate that I have had (and I have had quite a few)!My only real issue with it is that mine is gone and I will have to order some more.
Label: positive

Text: I've been a fan of Green & Black's Maya Gold, Organic Dark Chocolate with Orange & Spices, 3.5-Ounce Bars (Pack of 5) for many a year, so I was delighted to be offered Newman's Own Organics Dark Chocolate Bar, 3.25-Ounce Bars (Pack of 12) for review. (There is currently no link availble to the Pack of 12 2.25 ounce bars) I've been anxiously waiting for my package to arrive, as I had discovered a few recipes on the Newman's Own site, one of which is for a Chocolate Angel Cake that sounded just delightful - and a great way to use up some of that chocolate! Imagine my surprise when that Pack of 12 turned out to be a single 2.25 ounce bar!As a result, I really cannot comment on this particular product other than to say it has a nice, smooth chocolate taste, though it is fairly low in cocoa solids. Any other properties that Newman's Own Organics Dark Chocolate might have as a candy or confection coating or baking ingredient will remain untested. Strictly as a chocolate bar, this is one I can take or leave. Green & Black's - long involved in the organic, sustainable rainforest movement - will remain my go-to dark chocolate bar.
Label: neutral

Text: I really like NEWMAN'S OWN ORGANIC DARK CHOCOLATE BAR. It's not as bitter as some dark chocolates like Ghirardelli Chocolate Squares Dark Chocolate 60% Cacao, 5.25-Ounce Packages (Pack of 6) but that's due to the lower cocoa content, 54% vs. 60% and higher in others, so it can be a good or bad thing depending on how you like your dark chocolate to taste.For me, this is just right; not sweet like milk chocolate and not bitter at all! My only complaint would be that I wish the bar was scored so that the pieces would be a little bit smaller. Right now the bar is somewhat scored into six squares, but those are pretty big squares, so you have to break them in half to let it sit comfortably in your mouth and let it dissolve.That's really the best way to eat dark chocolate: put a small piece in your mouth and let it dissolve slowly . NEWMAN'S OWN DARK CHOCOLATE starts melting almost immediately into a chocolate cream that coats your tongue and the roof of your mouth, so that you get that wonderful taste just enough to satisfy a sweet craving.I highly recommend it to those who love chocolate, but want to skip the milk chocolate and find that other dark chocolates are too bitter. NEWMAN'S OWN DARK CHOCOLATE will satisfy that craving without making you go overboard.
Label: positive

Text: My wife and I independently taste-tested this product. My wife is a regular consumer of dark chocolates while I tend to prefer milk chocolate, and/or chocolates with fillings. After trying it out, we both decided that we would not purchase this product in the future, though for different reasons. Here are our independent reviews:My wife: I am confused by the labeling of this product. It is called a 'dark chocolate' but has a 54% cacao content. Most dark chocolates that I buy have upwards of 65% - 75% cacao. When I first opened the package, I noticed a smoky aroma. I eat a lot of dark chocolate, including raw chocolate, but I never noticed such a smoky aroma before and found it pleasant. My first bite was disappointing. It is a bit too sweet for me, and is certainly significantly sweeter than the dark chocolates that I buy. It is smooth, which I like. While I appreciate the fact that Newman's own is producing an organic, minimally processed, chocolate I will not purchase this again.Me: From my first bite I knew that this chocolate was not for me. It had a distinctly waxy feel that I usually associate with inferior chocolates - this was an immediate turnoff. There was absolutely nothing distinctive about this chocolate - no fruity notes, no particular bitterness, nothing that sticks out. While this chocolate is better than a lot of what constitutes the mass market, I cannot recommend it.
Label: negative

Text: Overall, this is a nice chocolate bar with the benefit of being organic and rainforest certified.  The chocolate dissolves quite smoothly and evenly - we compared it against a number of other dark chocolate bars we purchased from Trader Joe's and other places, and it dissolved more smoothly than any others.  It isn't as bitter as many other dark chocolates, and hence, it may appeal to some people who don't really like other dark chocolates.  The appearance of the bar was great - it had clean, dark surface, and there were no white blemishes or other marks that sometimes mar chocolate bars.  The bar breaks with a nice "snap," and the aroma is quite nice.  Overall, both my wife and I found the taste to be excellent.On the downside, the bar has fewer cocoa solids than the other cocoa bars we tasted - 54% cocoa solids.  Most of the others we sampled had 70%-80% cocoa solids - a couple were even higher.  This _could_ be important to some health-concious consumers, because many studies examining the health benefits of dark chocolate have defined "dark" to mean 65+% cocoa content.  That should be taken with a grain of salt, because some recent studies also have found merit to chocolates with lower cocoa content, but the majority of studies to date have focused on dark chocolate.Another possible concern related to this chocolate could be the price.  A twelve pack of bars comes in at $25,85 (possibly plus shipping); Trader Joe's has a number of bars that, although sometimes not as tasty, are certainly not as expensive.In sum, for taste, aroma, and presentation, this is a really good chocolate bar.  The negatives are price and a lower cocoa solid content.
Label: positive

Text: I don't usually like anything other than milk chocolate. But I do know that the benefits of dark chocolate are good, so I thought I would give this a whirl and I'm really glad I did.Much to my surprise I found this to be an altogether pleasant experience. This chocolate has a rich, creamy texture when allowed to melt slowly in your mouth. It also was wonderful just to down-right gobble, but I don't recommend it. (Gobbling that is) This product is just too good to gobble willy-nilly!It had no unpleasant bitter aftertaste that I've come to expect with certain darker chocolates. I sometimes like my chocolate to be a bit on the softer side, so I also tried the candy a tiny bit melted and found that it was even better. There is no waxy taste to this bar and it melts well, it is rich, creamy and full bodied. And I had to really restrain myself from gobbling this up in one sitting.I'm not one who likes European chocolates; I have a love of Hershey's and always will, so this product is right up my alley, and if I find myslef looking for something a little different...this will be it from now on.
Label: positive

Text: This is nothing like the Newman's Own chocolate I've had in the past! I don't know if they are changing all their chocolate products, after trying this I hope they are!Usually I'm not too crazy about dark chocolate & find that even the lesser percentage dark chocolates are still too strong for my liking.  This simply is not the case with this Signature Series bar!  It melts nicely in the mouth & isn't too bitter or too sweet.  The flavor is very well balanced & very satisfying.  After the first sniff & taste I thought I'd devour the entire bar; it was so good!  However, I enjoyed about half the bar & was amazed that my chocolate craving was soothed!Imagine that - chocolate left for another day!Last, I'd like to mention the ingredients because to me they are very important.  I would like to think that if you are considering Organic Chocolate, you care about the ingredients too.A big problem I have with the majority of Organic chocolate is the soy lecithin.  Ever notice that it's hardly ever organic?  Soy is a huge crop & I believe something like 80% of of it is GMO!  Big time Bad!  Thankfully this bar is ALL Organic:Organic evaporated cane juice, organic chocolate liquor, organic cocoa butter, organic soy lecithin, organic vanilla.  The Cocoa butter is Rainforest Alliance Certified.(A few Organic chocolate bars on the market contain NO soy lecithin!  Even better!)
Label: positive

Text: Newman's Own Organic Dark Chocolate Bar is good if you like semi-dark and mildly sweet chocolate that is a little harder with a slightly higher melting point than that of European chocolate of similar expectations. I liked the chocolate very much but I did not find it to have any superior qualities to Hershey's chocolate. The product is sold in a pack of 12 bars but my sample from the "Vine" program consisted of only one bar. I am not sure if my opinions would have changed given a larger sample, but my confidence and enjoyment of the review would have been significantly greater by having a larger sample from which to provide a better comparison of quality and quantity with competitive products. I am certain that the people running the Vine Program at Amazon fully understand the importance of having a reasonable sample in quantity and that such quantity that reached the reviewers may have been less than what was originally planned to be a sample of sufficient size. In other words, "Who ate the chocolate?" is a question that should be investigated by an independent party and that those with chocolate remnants on their fingers and lips should no longer be allowed to participate in management of the Vine Program.In summary, Newman's chocolate is good, but in my opinion nothing special that could justify the cost. But, the Newman products are supposed to be of higher quality with the excess margin being used to support worthwhile causes and I have no problem with a product such as chocolate where an inflated price provides excess margins to do some good that might otherwise may not have not been done.
Label: positive

Text: Newman's Own Organics Dark Chocolate bar is a tasty, fairly creamy chocolate bar.  One bite will satisfy your mid-afternoon chocolate craving and hopefully help you make it through the rest of the day.  Of course, any bite of chocolate around that time would probably do the same, so why choose this one?Its socially responsible.  The cocoa beans are grown on rainforest certified farms. From their website, "The Rainforest Alliance Certified(tm) seal guarantees that the cocoa in our Signature Series was grown on farms that meet comprehensive standards for sustainable farming, protecting soil, waterways and wildlife habitat as well as the rights and welfare of workers, their families and communities."Its organic.  Newman's Own is certified by Oregon Tilth.  If organic foods are important to you, Oregon Tilth certification is your guarantee that high standards have been met.Its simple.  Organic evaporated cane juice, organic chocolate liquor, organic cocoa butter, organic soy lecithin, and organic vanilla.Its yummy.  54% cocoa means its intensely chocolatey.  There is a hint of sweetness, but it retains the bitterness dark chocolate fans like.  Not too creamy, not too dry.Compared to a few other dark chocolate bars, Newman's Own stands up pretty well.  I did a little taste test with the following: Organic Dark Chocolate with Almonds - 3.5 oz Bar was the best, but it had an unfair advantage with the addition of almond bits. It was a little creamier than Newman's Own. Organic Dark Chocolate - Regular, 59% cocoa, 12 Units / 2 oz was almost indistinguishable from Newman's Own. Same texture and very similar flavor, perhaps slightly more intense. Trader Joe's Fair Trade Swiss Dark chocolate had 71% cocoa so it had a drier texture.  I even tried one by Ikea, which was inexpensive and chocolatey, but actually my least favorite.  It did not appear to be fair trade or organic.Overall, a good solid organic fair trade dark chocolate bar.  Dark chocolate fans will like it, milk chocolate fans might be converted.
Label: positive

Text: "Silky" is the word I kept coming back to as I tried this chocolate.  It's smooth, rich, and ... well, "silky" is just the right word.  Everything about this chocolate is decadent, but also feel-good insofar as it bears the 'organic' and 'rainforest certified.'  What does this mean in a practical sense to consumers?  From the Newman's Own site: "The USDA Organic Seal assures that at least 95% of the ingredients are organically certified." and "The Rainforest Alliance Certified(tm) seal guarantees that the cocoa in our Signature Series was grown on farms that meet comprehensive standards for sustainable farming, protecting soil, waterways and wildlife habitat as well as the rights and welfare of workers, their families and communities."Seals and certifications don't mean much in the mouth, however, and that's where this chocolate excels.  It's rich without being excessively bitter.  Smooth without lacking depth and substance.  It's easy to eat (and that comes from someone who struggles with chocolate, at times), and it's a very accessible dark chocolate.  A very adult alternative to the over-sweet milk chocolate one might find in a more pedestrian alternative.I don't know how better to celebrate this product than to just list the ingredients: Organic Dark Chocolate (organic evaporated cane juice, organic chocolate liquor, organic cocoa butter, organic soy lecithin, and organic vanilla).  This stuff is as feel-good as it is taste-good.  Can't wait to try the other varieties.
Label: positive

Text: Vine sent me this to review - I LOVE chocolate, especially dark chocolate so I was quite excited.  Now if you order this product you will get a pack of 12 - I got 1 so my only option was to simply consume it.  And I enjoyed that oh so much.  It had a harder texture than your hershey's special dark, but had a very similar flavor and sweetness.  It was very very tasty.  I would have liked additional bars to try things like baking with to fill you in on things like does it melt well in chocolate chip cookies or to make chocolate covered strawberries... I will have to guess on this as I didn't have enough to do that experiment.  Due to the harder nature of the chocolate I believe it would work well in cookies but not so much on strawberries.I like the idea of my chocolate being organic - I like where Newman's is going with their product line... I hope that they keep the word "Organic" meaning something other than just another sales pitch tactic.Anyway - if I'm in the market for Dark chocolate - I would pick this up without a second thought.
Label: positive

Text: After reading a number of articles on its possible health benefits, I became interested in finding a good organic dark chocolate to "take" instead of red wine (to provide polyphenols in my diet) and aspirin (for its blood-thinning properties). I appreciate the opportunity to review this 2.25-ounce 54% cocoa chocolate bar.One of these bars is divided into six squares. Two squares equals .75 ounces. According to a study published in the American Journal of Clinical Nutrition, half an ounce of dark chocolate was enough to boost antioxidant activity and reduce oxidation of LDL cholesterol. (I could not find the percentage of cocoa in the chocolate used in this study.) If these bars were divided into 8 instead 6, two sections would be closer to a half ounce serving, and this would bring the cost of a daily "medicinal" (:-Q) dose of chocolate down some. If these bars are priced comparably to the other organic chocolate bars in the Newman line, however, the .75-ounce serving of two squares will still cost less than a can of soda from a vending machine. If I'm not drinking red wine and taking aspirin, that's not going to break my bank account.Milk chocolate fans may not like the taste, but I like this chocolate a lot. It has just the right amount of bite in the finish without being bitter, and it melts slowly enough but not too slowly on the tongue. I'd like to also try the 70% cocoa bar to see which I would prefer. I'm after the health benefits more than the "sweets" aspect.Being Rainforest Alliance Certified, I can eat this chocolate without guilt, knowing no child slavery or destructive growing practices are involved with its manufacture.
Label: positive

Text: Unlike the other members of my family, I'm not a chocoholic.  I'm a take it or leave it consumer and in general, I go for the good stuff.  This sample bar was my introduction to the Newman's Own Organic Chocolate line, and in order to get a variety of opinions, I shared this taste with several co-workers.My immediate impression was that the bar had the nice, crisp snap of well-tempered chocolate, and also that it had a lovely texture, melting quickly and smoothly in my mouth.  The "dark" chocolate bar has a slightly smoky flavor, but only 54% cocoa, which is less than what many may be looking for in a dark bar.  (Newman's Own also has a super dark bar with 70% cocoa.)  All of us felt that this bar was sweeter than we would have ideally preferred, more akin to bittersweet chocolate than a true dark chocolate.  It was suggested, for that reason, that it might be a nice high-quality bar for baking--though as noted previously, it is not a standard baking bar size.The other thing that all of us noticed was a very subtle orange note to the flavor, to the point that we searched the ingredients for an explanation.  The ingredients of this organic bar are very simple and straightforward.  In fact, there are only five, none of which would explain this undertone.  And it wasn't a problem, overt, or unpleasant; it was simply commented upon.  Further perusing the Newman's Own offerings, I see they produce a dark chocolate orange bar, and I have to suppose there is some minor cross "contamination" of the flavors.  Mystery solved.A quick survey among my cohort revealed that there was only one of us who really cared that the product was organic.  Yeah, the rest of us are Philistines.  Based on the high price point and the low percentage of cocoa, even though we'd enjoyed the treat, none of us was inclined to purchase the product again.  Your mileage may vary.
Label: neutral

Text: Chocolate is by definition a Good Thing, dark chocolate doubly so.  And organic, Rainforest Alliance Certified dark chocolate?  Very nice.  It has a good flavor.  At 54% cocoa, it's not nearly as dark as some of the other bars on the market, and so is rather smooth on the tongue (I will dispute that it's waxy as one reviewer reported.)  It's true that it's not silky the way really fine chocolate is, but it doesn't purport to be.  It's the sort of chocolate you could find at your local supermarket.  High end there, but nowhere near the price or quality of a bar of Vosges organic dark chocolate.  Nor should it be compared to something like Vosges or even Godiva.  It's a good, affordable organic chocolate bar that has some good credentials in the environmental department.The bar is smallish, but I also debate the assertion that it's too small to share with a friend.  There are six good-size squares in this package, which are more than enough for two assuming that no one is being a glutton.  (That said, I admit I ate the whole bar myself today, but if there'd been anyone else here I'd have shared.)  Yeah, more is better when it comes to chocolate, but it's by no means necessary.Enjoy this chocolate bar for what it is.
Label: positive

Text: When the bar of NEWMAN'S OWN ORGANICS DARK CHOCOLATE arrived on our doorstep, it was a race to see who would devour it first. My daughters, my husband, our cat, and even our guests all lined up for a square of this delicious treat. But we had only one bar! So bit by bit, I doled out hunks of My Precious until the line had dissipated and only one hunk remained. So yes, I was the good hostess and the last to pop God's Gift to Chocoholics into my mouth.I should pause to say that I am not normally a huge fan of dark chocolate, but I will eat it in a pinch. This was not a pinch, but there I was, savoring a mouthful of truly delicious cocoa. What I generally dislike about dark chocolate is the bitter aftertaste, but with NEWMAN'S OWN ORGANICS DARK CHOCOLATE, they have managed to create a pleasantly sweet flavor that both mitigated the bitterness and complimented it.
