Sugars are 17 grams a bar; I've seen much worse. Chia seeds will give you a small amount of healthy fats and organic agave is the only sweetener. Downsides? The first two ingredients are not organic and they make up most of the bar. "Natural flavors" are used, never a good thing, and the cocoa is dutch processed which eliminates some antioxidant content. Other than that no real complaints. Nutrition grade: BALLERGENS: Made on shared equipment but labeled as gluten-free. No soy-derived ingredients that I could spot (watch out for those "natural flavors"). Obviously if you have a nut allergy you'll have to skip this line, but other than that the ingredient list is pretty straight-forward and clean.TASTE: This is easily the best tasting of the "Pure Naturals" series. It tastes like a gluten-free brownie, let's put it that way. Heat it up with a little coconut milk ice cream and you got a decent dessert. Not as yummy as the Zing Chocolate Coconut Bar, but better than a lot of the Larabars. Taste Grade: B/B-There you have it, hope that helps those of you desperate for a new bar that will work for your diet. Overall not bad tasting and decent ingredients. Happy snacking!FINAL GRADE: B/B-
Label: positive

Text: It is what it is. It's not a "chocolate bar" as the average American might think of. It is not a brownie. It is not a granola bar. It is a chewy bar composed of finely chopped almonds, dates, Dutch cocoa (which you can taste), Chia seeds, Fiber and sweetened with Agave Nectar.At 190 calories a bar, 8 grams of total fat, 25 grams of carbs, and 7 grams of protein, it makes for a healthful snack (or even breakfast). The fats are good ones, though.Our family was mixed in their reviews. My husband said he has tasted better bars at Sam's Club, I thought it was decent (though I wouldn't eat them regularly), my seven-year-old son loved them and was excited he would be eating the remaining ten bars, and my two-year-old daughter liked the bar as well.They are vegan (though we are not), and gluten-free. My son has a gluten sensitivity, so it is nice to have a snack he loves that would not aggrevate his body.Apparently these were founded by a lady who has a six-year-old vegetarian.Overall, they were good, but I don't know that they answer the claim on the box which reads, "Your chocolate prayers have been answered."
Label: positive

Text: Marketers know that if they slap "pure" and "natural" on anything, there's a segment of consumer that's automatically going to snarf them up.  Well, at least the people behind these bars seem to be doing it without cynicism.  They are gluten-free, vegan and the box they come in is even 100% recycled.  What's more, they taste pretty good as far as healthy snack bars go.I passed them out around the office to get a wide range of opinions.  Everyone liked the taste, and the bars were compared favorably to Clif Bars.  To me, they have some of the same chalky, dry texture as Clif Bars, although to a lesser degree.  No, this isn't going to win head-to-head with a brownie, but it is soft and mildly sweet and certainly healthier.  I ate one as a desert after lunch today and it was quite satisfying.Why would you want something vegan-friendly and gluten-free if you're not a vegan or a sufferer of Celiac disease?  Well, from the research I've seen, gluten messes with the gut of even people without celiacs.  So probably good to avoid.  And the vegan aesthetic, when done right, is better for the planet as a whole, vegans and non-vegans alike.  Win-win.Ingredients: dates, almonds, agave nectar, rice protein concentrate, cocoa (dutched), chia seeds, agave fiber, natural flavors.  Dates and agave are two of the healthiest sweeteners; almonds are supposedly one of the best nuts in terms of heart health; chia seeds are reputed to be good for skin.I try to stay low-carb.  You will get 25g of carbs in a bar, probably from the rice protein concentrate.  The other positives about the ingredients probably outweigh this factor for most people, but it is something else to be aware of.  A lot of sins can be hidden under the name "natural flavors" too, but I'm willing to give Pure Naturals the benefit of the doubt.Overall, this is the best snack bar I've encountered in terms of both ingredients and taste.  Eat responsibly.
Label: positive

Text: The wrapper forPure Natural Bars, Chocolate Almond, 1.7-Ounce Bars (Pack of 12) shows a bar with whole almonds and chunks of dark chocolate, so it was quite the surprise to open the wrapper to find a soft, squishy brown bar with pieces of almonds throughout.  Looks aside, I didn't find the taste of this all natural bar with 7 grams of protein and 5 grams of fiber to taste all that bad.However, without being told the ingredients, my children immediately picked up that there was "prune-like" fruit mixed in this chocolate bars and they turned up their noises at both the taste and the texture. (And they are not picky eaters)  Turns out that the first ingredient listed for this chocolate bar is dates.And, although the ingredients seem allergy friendly (ingredients are: dates, almonds, organic agave nectar, organic rice protein concentrate, cocoa, chia seeds, organic agave fiber and natural flavors), the package is clearly marked that the product is made in a facility that processes milk, eggs, wheat and peanuts. I think my friends with highly allergic kids would not take the risk of trying this product because of that fact.All that being said, I think my husband and I will enjoy having these bars around for a quick on-the-go breakfast or snack.
Label: positive

Text: These are supposed to be healthy vegan nutrition chocolate bars. If they tasted really good then I would ignore the calories and fat content. But they taste mediocre at best with a gritty texture which I suspect are the ground up fiber or almonds. The box shows whole almonds but there are no whole almonds in these bars. They also don't really have that rich dark chocolate taste either. I usually eat Kellog's Fiber Plus bars which actually taste quite good and definitely taste better than these Pure Naturals bars.When comparing Calories, Fat, Protein, Fiber and Vitamins, it is about a draw between the Pure Naturals and the Kellog's Fiber Plus. The Kellog's Fiber Plus bar has fewer calories per bar 130 vs 190, fewer fat calories 35 vs 70, more fiber 9g vs 5g and more Vitamin E and Zinc. But the Kellog's Fiber Plus also has more Saturated fat 3g vs 1g and fewer Proteins 2g vs 7g.Another problem is that the Pure Natural bars are promoted as Gluten-Free, Vegan and Natural Ingredients. I'll give them the Natural but they are processed in a plant that also processes Wheat, Peanuts, Soy, Sesame so that negates the Gluten-Free statement. The only plus I can find to these bars is that they use only natural or organic ingredients.I definitely would not call either bar healthy but if you have to have a chocolate bar then they are better for you than your typical chocolate bar. I would recommend trying the Kellog's Fiber Plus first and if not to your liking then try the Pure Naturals bars. Will I eat them, sure but will I buy the Pure Natural bars, not likely as they are very costly compared to the Kellog's Fiber Plus.
Label: negative

Text: I had heard a little about this product from the local pet store, then tried a small bag for our 2 cats of about 3 and 6 years respectively. The female, younger one took to it right away, and the older male, who was unfortunately weaned on the junk cat food with all the corn meal and etc... in it, and would usually refuse a diet change, actually liked it too. The older male is overweight and we were trying to switch him to soft food on the doctor's request, but he totally refused it. He is a posh cat after all. He still consumes too many calories, the fatty, but at least I know he's eating good food. Of course, I know this product seems to cost more than most other feline food on the market, but I just can't see feeding our animals the lousy alternatives that are out there, marketed as being healthy. Why do commercials show cats eating a vegetarian diet which is mostly corn meal? Is that right? I'll stay with this for now, but stay tuned.I'm updating this review after 9 months of using this cat food for our two cats. I upgraded the stars from 4 to 5 as it really has been a good food for the two of them. Garfield still eats a little too much, but he's just a big cat who doesn't exercise much, let's face it. Our female cat will be 4 years old in a couple months and she's so healthy. She's tiny, but happy and agile. I tried the chicken version of Holistic Select, but Garfield seemd to miss this sardine version so we went back to this one. I've ordered the duck version and will update this once they try it.
Label: positive

Text: I have been using Giovanni Body Wash (White Tea scent) for several years now. I have extremely sensitive skin, prone to acne, rashes and irritations when using most other soaps. I have wasted tons of money trying soap after soap hoping to find something that wouldn't irritate my skin, lathering well, while leaving me feeling clean. This soap has a really nice, soft scent, washes skin clean of dirt and oil and leaves no soap scum after rinsed off. This is the only soap I have found that does not cause break-outs/irritations and lathers well. I truely wish I could find a more econonmcal product that does the same job as this body wash but still haven't found one. So I try to find really good deals when buying this body wash to help off set the cost.Please note: this soap is a little drying.. so if you already have a dry skin conidtion this may not be the soap for you. I lived in a very warm humid climate when I started using this soap and did not notice the drying effect until I moved to a much cooler and dryer area. With that being said Giovanni also sells one of the best moisturizers I have ever used, it's their body butter (comes in the same scents as the body washes). This body butter works fantasticly! I am in the health care field and have to wash my hands many, many times a day. If I put some of the body butter on at night right before I go to sleep, my hands are moisturized all the next day without reapplying! I have tried many moisturizers that seems to work great until you wash your hands, then its like you never had any on in the first place... the body butter is not like this.
Label: positive

Text: Ok, so I absolutely love the packaging and the nifty description on the back of the bottle, but when I'm paying .64/oz, I expect it to moisturize. Giovanni claims that their washes include "essential oil complex to soothe, smooth and regenerate skin's lost moisture" but the ingredients' list contains no such ingredient, unless they are referring to the "Essential Oil Fragrance" in which case they are blatantly lying because essential oils provide scent, not moisture. Even the dollar bottles of cheapo wash at Walmart contain some type of moisturizer, albeit petroleum. Sometimes I use St. Ives Whipped Silk body wash and it's definitely more moisturizing than Giovanni due to the sunflower oil. I've noticed even JASON's washes contain Sodium PCA to moisturize (although their stuff also has harsh sulfates, so I've never tried them). Or maybe Giovanni deliberately left out any moisturizers out so that we buy their lotions/body butters. Ha!While I sound like I hate the product, I really don't; I just wish it provided some moisture. The good: it looks and feels like a spa product, washes well and rinses cleanly, and smells good and not artificial perfumey like Herbal Essences and all that other junk. Also, it actually works well as a shampoo when in a pinch. In fact, I've taken to using it as shampoo as well since Giovanni keeps changing their shampoo formulas (and for the worse, it seems, judging from the new ingredients) and body washes tend to be more gentle than shampoos. I've tried Raspberry Winter, Lavender Vanilla Snow, Cassifluer White Tea, and D:tox (bar soap form) and they all smell awesome. True aromatherapy in a bottle.
Label: positive

Text: I have been needing to give up coffee due to the acidity. I have tried several "low acid" coffees as well as made my own "Cold brew" coffee concentrate. Nothing was quite what I needed where flavor was concerned, and most were still upsetting my stomach. I had already given up the caffeine, so that was not an issue. When I saw the favorible reviews of the Teeccino products, I decided to give them a try. I sampled these "Tee Bags" first, as they came in a small box, so I figured if I did not like them, I wasn't out much.I cannot believe how much I LOVED this product! The Tee Bags make it super easy to prepare, and the aroma was great from the moment I tore open the little envelope. The flavor really is wonderful! Very rich and satisfying! I added a little flavored creamer to mine, just as I would with coffee and it was delicious. I am very surprised at just how much this tasted like coffee. It is my new favorite product. I highly recommend it to anyone having to give up coffee. I am looking forward to trying the "ground, brew your own" version, as well as the other flavors.
Label: positive

Text: I just bought my first box of this, being a sort of STASH CHAI addict, drinking the BLACK CHIA TEA, and the GREEN CHIA TEA, both of which are good. So, you like CHAI? great. The box lists the same spices for each box, but they are in different ratios, depending on the strenght of the backround tea. The black tea, uses ginger, then cinnamon, clove, allspice, nutmeg, and cardamon, in decreasing amounts of spice, as you decend down the spice list. (altho the company also uses spice oils, for the teas, for more flavor.) The green tea CHIA as more cinnamon in it, compaired to the black chia. NOW, for the WHITE CHIA. SPICES AS  FOLLOWS, its mostly CINNAMON, then ginger, allspice, clove, nutmeg, and cardamon. ALL real CHIA teas, use these spices, but they need to be balanced to the teas, and WHITE TEA, is very delicate tea, of course. ALso, since it is even less processed than GREEN TEA, its very easy to make it bitter.  (It has more antioxidants than green tea too.) SO, if you get this, and you want it to taste good, dont throw BOILING WATER on the tea bag(s). Let your water sit for a minute, until its around 190 degrees F. THEN, add your tea bags, and dont let it brew for more than FOUR MINUTES. THEN, like ALL CHIA TEA, it must be VERY sweet, for the spices to "work". I add asparteme, and LOTS of it ,and a bit of honey, and some SOY MILK....ALL DONE!!! Stash's chia tea is the best, the VERY BEST. I tasted them all. There's no black pepper, chicory, or other wierdness.  SO, even tho with white chia you only get 18 bags of tea per box, instead of 20 with the GREEN or BLACK CHIA, its worth the few extra pennies for this tea. BUT, first, do you like WHITE TEA, and DO YOU LIKE SPICES? (and if you dont like to sweeten your tea, or add milk/soy, just stay away from CHIA. ) PS. dont make this the old TIBETIAN way, cos you dont want to BOIL white tea.
Label: positive

Text: Trader Joe's has been carrying the No Pudge Brownie mixes for over a decade and it's not unusual to see customers buying them by the case.  At the current price of $15.55 for 6 that makes them $2.59 a pack if you have Amazon prime or use Super Saver Free shipping and that's not a bad deal.  What mnakes these brownies so popular is that all you have to add is yogurt.  I add Vanilla Nonfat yogurt and sometimes I'll toss in two tablespoons of instant coffee of espresso powder to jazz them up.  You don't need butter or eggs - just the yogurt.  Once you do that, you stir 'em up, pour 'em in the pan and in no time you have delicious brownies!  They aren't as ooey-gooey as some mixes but that's what I like about them.  They also aren't cloyingly sweet.  They are simply a nice, fudgey brownie that you don't have to feel too guilty about eating. I always keep a couple packages in the pantry to whip up for unexpected company or for whenever that chocolate craving hits you out of nowhere.  The best thing about No Pudge Fudge Brownies is that they are easy to mix, quick to cook and they always come out to be the same good and fudgey brownie you are longing for. Keep some on hand at all times!
Label: positive

Text: I don't typically buy foods that are advertised as non-fat for precisely the reasons that a previous reviewer mentioned--they're gummy, barely taste like what they're supposed to taste like, and just generally disgusting.  I also don't like fat-free foods because of all the chemicals and added sugar that usually goes into them to give them something resembling their full-fat texture.This product really is different from all those other fat-free products, though, and I genuinely enjoy it.  The ingredients are all things I typically would keep in my cupboard without feeling the need for a biohazard sticker on the door (if only I had time to bake).The fact that one can make an easy single-serving brownie in the microwave is an added bonus for all of us who don't live with a big family of people who will eat our homemade creations, or who simply don't have time to make a pan of homemade brownies.  I concede that these are not as good as a gourmet fudge brownie, but they are precisely what I'd hoped they'd be when I got them: yummy, quick, easy, economical, and above all chocolatey.My favorite serving suggestion: drop a few chocolate chips in the cup before putting them in to bake.
Label: positive

Text: It was 11 pm on a Monday.  Someone sent me an e-mail detailing the waffles and berry sauce she was going to prepare for a weekend brunch.  My mouth began to water just as I was getting ready to head to bed.  Darn it!A subletter in my student apartment had left No Pudge brownie mix in my cabinet, and when I came back from the summer break, I almost threw out the box when I saw it.  I typically don't buy anything low-fat or non-fat, as I prefer to recognize the ingredients in whatever food I purchase and eat.  (The "everything in moderation" motto of my 88-year-old grandmother has worn off.  For my healthy under-30-year-old self, a little butter is fine in a fruit and veggie-rich diet short on processed foods.)Anyway, there was nothing around to satisfy my sudden must-have-chocolate attack.  The pink box called out to me from the back of the cabinet, and I winced.  No Ghirardelli in sight.  Low-fat chocolate?  Well, if I must.  I figured the brownies would be horrible but satisfy a craving, and that I could then pitch the rest of the pan.Not the case!  The craving hit at 10:55 pm, and I was cutting the brownies (having added *regular* Brown Cow plain yogurt with a tsp+ of natural vanilla) at 11:40.  They were really good warm.  The next day, completely cooled, they were even better!  I actually think that that chocolate taste of these is much better than any brownie mix I've ever encountered.  With the yogurt I used, the consistency was more cake-like than chewy, but I'll deal with that minor shortcoming.  These were awesome, and I'll be buying a supply of my own!
Label: positive

Text: I read an article about No Pudge brownies somewhere and made a mental note to try it out someday. My grocery store now carries them so I thought I'd give them a try. I use fat-free plain yogurt and two drops of vanilla extract (which honestly doesn't really make a difference at all if you don't have any; I've tried both ways) and they still come out faaabulous! I make them all the time for girls' night-in; it's always a favorite! In my house, these brownies have never seen the light of dawn -- they go fast. :) I love sweets and baked goods but I stay away from them to keep in shape. I'm serious when I say that I don't have to worry with these brownies (even if I splurge on a couple extra pieces..out of 12). For some reason, I don't gain an inch! They're filling yet light, moist, rich, and are composed of the perfect texture ..not being too chewy, dry, or cake-like. Make sure not to over-bake them to the point that the top gets lighter and dry. If you see it start to happen and your brownies aren't ready, cover the top of your pan carefully with aluminum foil and it should be fine. I recommend this to everyone. I hope they never go out of business haha.. I plan on using their mix for life.
Label: positive

Text: I've lost 89 lbs since January of 2010, and I wanted to say that these have been a staple of my dieting. I'm not saying this was the only dessert I had but I have to say that it was one I had most frequently and still continue to have!  My favorite way and practically the only way I've served this was the single serve. I'd take 4 large tablespoons, sometimes 5 of the mix, and then I'd add 2 tablespoons of unsweetened applesauce.  I felt that this would help combat the extra mix and guarentee this was not adding any extra calories or fat that the yogurt sometimes has, and more importantly, no extra sugar. I'd add this to a microsafe coffee mug.  Once in a while, I'd add a few extra treats: a teaspoon of chocolate chips, or a teaspoon of peanut butter (which DOES add fat), and sometimes if I have any, a very small spoonful of ice cream.  And I ALWAYS add a small dose of low fat whipped cream to the top.  Part of dieting means treating yourself, and while I have this sometimes a few days a week, I never noticed a weight gain while consuming this.  This stuff is wonderful!!
Label: positive

Text: Even non-dieters love these.  1 box, made as per instructions (I use a 6oz container of nonfat vanilla yogurt usually) makes 1 pan, but I find that it makes a decently thick brownie if you use an 8x8 pan and adjust cooking time accordingly.  I couldn't spread the whole batter out into a larger pan. The batter is very thick and when all you are adding to the box of powder mix is a small carton of yogurt, it seems unlikely that that small amount of yogurt will turn a bowl of powder into a bowl of batter but if you keep stirring, it does happen. I also tried it using nonfat plain Greek yogurt plus a tsp of vanilla but it was too thick so I had to add a few tablespoons of water and then the batter came out perfect and the brownies were just as great as always. I like that you can do single servings in a bowl in the microwave (I double these as they are pretty small).  These brownies are not as gooey and flakey as a regular decadent type but they have more a thicker chewy-ness (like a crust but not hard or flaky) on the bottom and outside edges that some people prefer.  For a nice treat, top these brownies with fat free Cool Whip and some sugar free chocolate syrup... diet brownie heaven. They sell this mix in my local supermarket and it's about $3-$5 depending on if there's a sale.  These brownies are very sticky when cutting them so it's also good to make them in muffin tins instead of brownie pans.
Label: positive

Text: Here's a review by someone who is not fat, not on a diet, and who has not ruined his palate with fat free confections:These are the worst brownies I have ever tasted!  My wife bought this mix because it was the only one available at Trader Joe's when she shopped.  She didn't tell me they were fat free, but as soon as I tried one, I knew.  It had all the hallmarks of fat-free baked goods - gummy, flat, and the chocolate tasted like cocoa powder, not like rich fudgy chocolate.  I almost had to spit it out, it was that awful.And do you want to know why fat free foods taste so bad?  I'll let you in on a little secret:  the secret ingredient in good-tasting food is... you guessed it - FAT!!!!!  Fat and oil molecules transport the lovely taste of foods onto your taste buds.  So stop being so afraid of fat!  A little bit won't kill you!  Just exercise a little self-control and don't eat the entire box.  One properly made brownie is more satisfying than a whole plate of these vile things.So if you like real food that tastes good, don't believe the reviewers that raved about these brownies.  These people have a hidden agenda - they are trying to convince themselves that these little nuggets of evil actually taste good and are good for them.
Label: negative

Text: I bought this product due to all the good reviews.  I bbq using a weber smokey mountain often.  I usually make my own rubs.  I thought this might be a good product to keep on hand in case I didn't feel like making a rub.Most good bbq rubs have a base of salt and brown sugar.  This rub does not contain any sugar.  It is however very heavy on the salt.  I am used to using a large amount of rub when bbq to get a nice bark.  I made some ribs using this rub and they were so salty they were almost inedible.  I tasted the rub before hand and cut it with some brown sugar but clearly not enough.I have used this rub on other foods and had better results.  A little is very delicious on french fries or corn on the cob where I use this in place of salt.If you intend on using this rub heavily for low and slow cooking plan on cutting it heavily with brown sugar.  I prefer a salty spicy rub over an overly sweet rub but this is too much on its own.  I just wish they used more of the other ingrediantes and less salt as the spice is very tasty.If you plan on using this to spice up other foods in place of salt you may be very happy with this.
Label: positive

Text: An Update 6/29/2011:  We like this product enough that we are placing another order for 5 packages.  Of course this product cannot cure our dog, but because of this product, he acts like he is not hurting so much and we have put off the surgery.  We still haven't found a better price than that offered on Amazon.Original review 1/15/2011:  Our 9 pound schnauzer started limping, then his hind leg started collapsing inward at the knee. Our vet took x-rays which showed--in layman's terms--knee joint problems on both hind legs.  He said it was caused by "genetics".  We love this dog so much, but corrective surgery is in the thousands (per knee).  While we decide on a future course-of-action, our vet prescribed anti-inflammitory pills and Glyco-Flex III Mini Bite-Sized Chews.  Our dog loves these chews.  We tell him to come and get a "treat"--and he believes that it is and comes running.  Yes, running.  He is no longer taking the anti-inflammatory medication, but continues with the chews.  At the moment, his leg is no longer collapsing, but we can tell that the knee with the biggest problem is still painful and stiff--but not like it was just weeks ago.  This stuff will not solve our dog's problem, but it sure seems to have helped.  We are going to continue buying these chews for our dog--and if you do some comparative shopping, you will find that Amazon's price is almost half of what you'll see elsewhere.
Label: positive

Text: I normally get this tea at Teavana stores in the mall or their website, so was excited to see it on Amazon (as I have tons of gift cards here). It seemed to take a little longer then other products to arrive but came in great shape and came with a free sample just like my other orders from the Teavana site have (which makes sense since they're partnered with Amazon). I drink the White Ayurvedic Chai with the  Samurai Chai Mate and they taste fantastic together. They're supposed to be full of caffeine but for me they're my anytime tea. I have to steep a little longer then recommended time as I like my tea strong (with a bit of sugar or honey :) but it tastes great. Not to sickly sweet like some fruity teas.It is sweeter then some of their other teas, so if you don't like sweet then you may want to try out some of their others. I recommend going into the stores and sampling their array of teas they usually have set out to find your favorite. The blend they normally have of this one is good but not always what comes out at home though so don't just go by that one. They use German rock sugar (which they sell) and that costs a little more. Most people will use normal table sugar, honey, or artificial sweetener which will affect the tastes. Anyway, go try the tea, find your tea and drink happy.
Label: positive

Text: Kellogg's Sunshine Cheez-IT Mozzarella Cheese, 13.7-Ounce (Pack of 4)I sent for Kellogg's Sunshine Cheez-IT Mozzarella Cheese through my Vine offerings for review from Amazon. However, since the ingredients were not visible, I was unaware that the product contained MSG (monosodium glutamate) to which my entire family, me included, is allergic. So, I asked my neighbor if any of his household was allergic or sensitive to MSG-monosodium glutamate and he said no. So, they became my test group for the CHEEZ-IT with Mozzarella. Three boys high school and college age and their father, they reported as follows:21 year old: "Excellent Mozzarella flavoring. I liked it with ham added as a cracker snack."19 Year old: "Wow! Pete thanks, we all liked it and like my older bro I too made cracker and ham sandwiches of it!"12 year old: "it was good but a little too salty for me, so my brothers and dad ate most of it."Dad: "It was good and I joined the boys only I added an Italian ham to it, the spicy Capicola and it was great."There you have it, only one hitch, a suggestion to Kelloggs, RE: I read said that, more than 27% of people are sensitive to or allergic to MSG (monosodium glutamate), so any company or restaurant that uses it is automatically shutting off a sizable segment of their market.I agree, why cut out ANY of your market just to use a cheap substitute for spices'? I give it 3.5 stars which rounds out to four stars above, I took off 1.5 stars for the MSG added because it limits the marketability of the product and because we could not eat it.
Label: negative

Text: Cheez-IT crackers are a great little snack - easy to eat, full of flavor and with a good shelf-life they are easy to keep on hand. Although I'd mostly stuck to the Cheez-It Baked Snack Crackers, Original, 13.7-Ounce Boxes (Pack of 4) flavor, a sale at Kroger's Grocery Store last year had me experimenting with some of the other flavors. Although the Cheez It Baby Swiss Crackers, 13.7-Ounce Boxes (Pack of 4), Cheez-It Baked Snack Crackers, Duoz, Sharp Cheddar & Parmesan, 13.7-Ounce Boxes (Pack of 4), and Cheez-It Baked Snack Crackers, White Cheddar, 13.7-Ounce Boxes (Pack of 4) flavors were good, my particular favorites were the Cheez-It Snack Mix, Baked Snack Assortment, 10.5-Ounce Boxes (Pack of 3) and Cheez-It Baked Snack Crackers, Italian Four Cheese, 13.7-Ounce Boxes (Pack of 4) flavors.Now Kellogg's has added a new Mozzarella Cheez-Its flavor, and I couldn't resist trying them. I have to say they aren't bad, but they aren't even close to being my favorite flavor. I think one of my favorite parts of the Cheez-Its experience is the bold flavor in their crackers, and the Mozzarella flavor is a bit too mild to suit me. Like I said, they aren't bad, but I wouldn't say they are great.Of course, something like flavor is a really subjective thing. Other people may prefer the mild flavor of the Mozzarella Cheez-Its to the bolder tastes in their other offerings.In all honesty, after discovering the Italian Four Cheese flavor, no other Cheez-It really comes close for me, with the exception of the Snack Mix, which is really completely different from just a Cheez-It.Do I recommend the Mozzarella Cheez-Its? I think they are an interesting flavor and are worth trying yourself. Everyone's tastes are different, and although I prefer the other flavors, you might very well consider the Mozzarella your own favorite.
Label: positive

Text: I love cheez-Its from the get go but these mozzarella cheese ones are truly scrumptious.  They claim they are creamy...... and they really are creamy tasting.  From the first cracker in your mouth you think 'creamy'.  Sooo DELICIOUS!  There is 180 mg of sodium per each of 13 servings/box at 25 crackers per serving.  They do have 7 grams of fat/serving but no trans fats.  From a diabetic point of view, the 19 grams of carbohydrates and 150 calories/serving is a lot even with zero sugars and 3 grams of protein per serving.  I try to restrict my consumption to one or two serving each time I grab the box for a snack but I have to admit this is really hard to do.  They just taste so darn great!  If I have a complaint, the only one I have is that this product contains monosodium glutamate, MSG.  I am highly allergic to this well known neurotoxin and MUST be very careful with my intake of this dangerous chemical.  It is on the very bottom of the ingredients list so hopefully there is not a hugh amount of it in these crackers.  Since I just ate them, I'll know if there is too much MSG if I develop a headache and lock jaw type symptoms tomorrow.  Makes me wonder WHY put it into this delicious snack in the first place?  I do highly recommend this scrumptious treat if you can tolerate the MSG.  I'll have to limit my intake due to the MSG.
Label: positive

Text: My OLD favorite, was the "Original" flavor Cheeze-It, which had a strong cheddar flavor, and lots of crunch.My NEW favorite is the Mozzarella flavor Cheese-it, because it has a true mozzarella cheese taste, as well as that old time crunch that everybody in my family loves.We generally eat the Original flavor as a snack right out of the box, or along with soup (tomato soup, in particular).The Mozzarella flavor as a plain snack, right out of the box, was good, but we still prefer the original, (thus the 4 stars) but I used them in  the following ways as well:--topped a caesar salad with a few crackers (in place of croutons, and that worked great, taste-wise.-- topped off some tomato soup with a few crackers at the dinner table, and that didn't work quite as well, taste wise. --crushed approx. 2/3 cup  of these and used them as a bread crumb coating for a baked chicken breast parmesan, topped with tomato sauce. Very good, since the mozzarella flavor really came through.Nutritionally, and calorically, they are about the same as the original flavor.All in all, I'd say if you like the old, original Cheeze-It flavor, you'll enjoy the new mozzarella flavor too.
Label: positive

Text: It's certainly a fun exercise trying to come up with spin-off products that are as popular or more popular than the original product. Such mainstays as Diet Coke and Honey Nut Cheerios are usually the only ones I can think of before I A.D.D. myself off to another topic.What was I talking about?Oh:  Mozzarella Cheez-Its.  I've been eating Cheez-Its since I was a kid, and it's usually better to not have a box around as it's hard for me to stop eating them.  "White Cheddar" Cheez-Its are, so far, the only spin-off product that I like as much as the original, and these new Mozzarella Cheez-Its come in a close second.  With a similar look and feel to the White Cheddar variety, they do indeed have a slightly more "creamy & mild" (as advertised on the box) taste, but, frankly, aren't too terribly different. The ingredients even show "white cheddar cheese" higher up on the list than mozzarella!No worries, though.  We don't buy this kind of product for its old-school Italiano cheese authenticity, nor, certainly, for its healthful benefits (btw, fat, salt and other bad things are similar to the other Cheez-It varieties). We buy it because we like the way it tastes, and those that like regular Cheez-Its and the White Cheddar variety as their guilty snack cracker pleasure won't be disappointed.
Label: positive

Text: I have been a fan of Cheez-it crackers since my teen days when I enjoyed the cheddar cheese Cheez-its.  We used to sit in front of the TV with a bowl of Raspberry Apple Sauce and a box of Cheez-its.  Today I just received a new box, this one is Mozzarella Cheez-its, wow these are really a treat.  The flavor is much the same with a little subtle flavor of Mozzarella.Cheez-its, are made with 100% real cheese and you can taste it, the flavor is very fresh and creamy.I do not detect any MSG, although it is in there, and I find that the sour taste that was noted is not (to me) sour but a bit tangy, it gives it the zip you would expect in a nice cheesy snack cracker.  We all have different tastes and I find this one is a winner for mine.I love them.For the health conscious here are the nutritional facts:Serving size: 25 crackersServings per box: 13Total fat 7g  with no trans fatsPoly. Fats 3.5gMonio. Fats. 1.5Cholesterol: 0Sodium 180Total Carbs. 19gSugars 0Protein 3gAll in all I think this is a very nice baked flavorful crispy snack cracker.I am enjoying them very much, and I think you will also.My only problem now is to stop shoveling them into my mouth.Enjoy
Label: positive

Text: In our household, we often purchase Cheez-It baked snack crackers and we often like to purchase various cheese flavors.My son absolutely loves Cheez-It's and often, we purchase the White Cheddar or original versions, but we noticed there was a new Mozzarella "Creamy & Mild" version that is available and is made with 100% real cheese.And for anyone who loves Mozzarella, the taste of these crackers are flavorful and as our 9-year-old would say "very yummy"!For those who are wondering about the nutrition facts, for 25 crackers, it's about 150 calories, cholesterol is 0mg and 0% and total fat is 11% with saturated fat at 8%.  Ingredients contains wheat, milk and soy.Now, purchasing Cheez-It are typically affordable but surprisingly a four-pack of a 13.7 ounce goes for nearly $30 and for a single box, they cost nearly $12.  For me, that is just insane...unless it's specially imported from Italy or if it was some huge family-sized package as big as family-sized cereal box, I can easily see how $12 is justified but for this small 13.7 ounce version to cost around $12 and a 4-pack for $30 just doesn't make sense (I am assuming that these third party sellers are factoring in huge shipping costs).JUDGMENT CALL:Sunshine's Cheez-It's are delicious and like other various flavors, next to our favorite of White Cheddar, Mozzarrella "Creamy & Mild" is going to be the version we will most likely purchase... but as long as you can purchase Cheez-It's for under $5, it's hard to justify these being sold for $12 or $30 for four 13.7 oz. boxes, these are costing more than Crunchmasters and other crackers that are sold at Trader Joes and even speciality crackers that I have purchased in Tokyo.Overall, Sunshine's Mozzarella Cheez-It's are wonderful!  Too bad I can't say so much about the price!
Label: positive

Text: I wasn't sure what to expect from this product.  It has a lot of packaging and I suspect one could do this basic thing at home with a box of cake mix and a jar of ice cream topping; but still, this is rather cool.All you do is open the package, put the powder in the bowl, add 1/4 cup water and mix it, and squeeze the sauce packet on top.  Put in microwave for 1 minute and 15 seconds.  That's it.  I have been adding nuts to mine before cooking and that works great.  What results is basic chocolate cake with a mild caramel sauce squiggled on it.  The warmth factor is what makes this so appealing; it really seems to add a nice and satisfying quality to it.  Also, while it's definitely sweet, it isn't as sweet as I was worried it would be.  You could choose to use less of the caramel sauce if you wanted, of course, or add other ingredients like I do; I'm going to try coconut and berries, but just a few nuts is really great.I don't think I would buy this again simply because I feel like the packaging is overkill and I do want to try replicating this process with a plain box of cake mix.  But it does taste good, and it works effectively and quickly.
Label: positive

Text: The pet food industry can be one of the most infuriating as you start doing research and discovering what trash manufacturers (and the vets who get paid off by the manufacturers) push onto the unsuspecting public. For reference, don't ask your vet - do some Internet research at sites like, for example, [...]. What you'll find is that even the supposed "high-end" lines from basically every major supermarket label are horrible for your fuzzy friends. People who trust the marketing or trust their vets overpay and feed their cats foods that are full of carcinogens, cheap ingredients that are inappropriate for a cat's digestive system, and byproducts that provide nothing of value and are basically a way to make cheap profit off of literal garbage. It's a tragedy, as these same cats end up with shorter lifespans, kidney problems, UTIs, cancer, and a host of other issues, all of which could have been avoided by a high quality food.This is an A-Grade food. What you'll find here is very high protein content, many meats high on the list, flaxseed, good vitamins and antioxidants, and no grains, which is great as a cat is not an omnivore, it's a carnivore, and it simply does not need grains.What you will NOT find here is the kind of junk you'll see in things like Purina One and Science Diet - byproducts (aka beaks, feathers, bird feet), brewers rice (aka the waste product from making beer), wheat flour (a leading cause of pet allergies), corn gluten (a cheap waste of space), and things like "liver flavor" (because they don't want to pay for the real thing). I didn't just pull those ingredients out of the air - I was reading the ingredients list of Purina One. It's pathetic.Don't feed your cats trash. Pay a little more to feed them good food now, and they'll lead longer, healthier, more satisfied lives as a result. It's worth it.
Label: positive

Text: The school where I work has a "popcorn day" one Friday per month.  The popcorn is popped in a theater-type popper.  They use the Act II Buttery flavored oil and the Act II butter flavored salt.  I don't know how others who gave such poor reviews are popping their corn, but our popcorn is fabulous!  It is a huge hit at school, both with the kids and the adult staff.  We have families who buy enough for every member of the family for a movie and popcorn night.  Maybe others are not measuring it correctly, or are not using the right popper.  I don't know.  All I can say is I love this stuff!!  I smell it being popped all day and can't wait to eat it!!  It takes all my willpower to keep from eating a bag of it for lunch!If you use the Act II salt with the oil, be careful not to use too much of the salt.  It seems to be concentrated.  It doesn't take as much as you might think.If I could have these people send me their supply that they obviously don't know how to use, I'd be in heaven!I'm buying myself a theater-style popper for Christmas, just so I can have this popcorn more often than once a month!
Label: positive

Text: IN A NUTSHELL: FEW NATURAL FOODS COULD BE WORSE FOR YOU THAN LARD!WHAT IS WORSE?  --- Hydrogenated LARD, which is loaded with TRANS FATTY ACIDS - CHOLESTEROL - SATURATED FAT - AGRICULTURAL CHEMICALS - HORMONES ETC. AND that's what this product is.SOME INTERESTING STUFF: WHERE IS THE LARD ON THEIR WEBSITE?Dial, the soap people, own Armour, and Armour makes this Hydrogenated, "All Natural" Lard. However, though Armour owns up to making many less than healthy edibles, on their website, there is NO mention of Lard as one of their many products.If you have to use Lard, this is a long established brand that resturants use, and though "hydrogenated", this is a good product as hydrogenated Lard goes.Nevertheless, I wince when I leave a resturant, and while walking through their parking lot find myself staring at huge tubs of empty lard containers lying around the dumpsters. Funny, but I'd swear they weren't there before I ate.BOTTOM LINE:This is not just lard, but Hydrogenated Lard, which should be stricken from the human diet if at all possible. If that is not possible, Armour has been a reliable brand that has been making lard forever, though they don't want to admit it apparently [WONDER WHY?].It does, however, make me wonder what is in Dial soap?![I AM NOT AFFILIATED WITH ANY PRODUCT OR COMPANY]
Label: negative

Text: I love my coffee--in any way shape or form. I especially love to add espresso shots to a regular cup of coffee. But one thing I've never done is have espresso over ice.This product had sugar added (beet sugar) so it is NOT made for purists and a web search did not give me the info I wanted as to how much caffeine this product has.What it does have is an excellent flavor that was unexpected from a canned product and a convenience that I find gratifying.Take the warning on the back of the can seriously--do NOT shake this product before using, it actually isn't necessary (I shook it a little to see why there was a warning and it frothed all over the place when I opened it).- if you don't have issues other than not wanting milk in your iced espresso have no fear, if there was milk in it I couldn't tell.Delicious, flavorful, not overly bitter -just enough to give a kick, convenient.I will definitely buy more of this product! At a 1.92 per nearly a 7oz can - and with a 20% off coupon when you buy from Amazon, this is a pretty good deal for the convenience of it all!
Label: positive

Text: Obvious yes, given that this is an 'Italian Espresso'style coffee drink."It isn't too bad!"  VERY high praise from my husband, seeing as he doesn't even like coffee.But I do, and while this had a richer flavor than I was expecting, I am not a huge fan of the aftertaste.I am no espresso connoisseur, in fact that only espresso I have ever had is Starbucks, so I don't have a whole lot to compare it to, but I do like strong coffee.  A slightly bitter taste is actually something I enjoy, as opposed to overly sweetened coffee that seem to be the rage.  This little can (6.8 FL OZ) certainly has that.  It has a surprisingly bitter taste with just the right amount of sweetness.  At 50 calories a can, it's relatively diet friendly too.But there, at least for me, was something of a strange film in my mouth.  Not a big deal, but at almost $2 per can ($22.93 for 12) I expect to like it much more than I did.  If I was running errands and really wanted some iced coffee, I might consider this, but at home I will stick with my own brew.
Label: positive

Text: As a devout drinker of good, fresh roasted/ground/drip brewed, strong coffee, I approached this can of ILLYissimo espresso with much skepticism and profoundly low expectations. The experience of drinking it caught me by complete surprise as it is entirely acceptable as a cold/cool glass of coffee on the run!Described by another reviewer as "muddy water in a can", I can only infer that strong coffee is not to that person's liking. This IS a strong formulation and does not suffer from the burnt taste or bitter aftertaste that many other canned coffee beverages have. It is sweetened, but mildly so and used beet sugar to do this.  Too many other canned coffee beverages are overly sweetened thereby destroying any vestige of honest coffee flavor.Made in Denmark and distributed by Coca-Cola, it is a pleasure to find an alternative to either sickeningly sweet or simply bad coffee in cans or bottles.Rich, satisfying and easily portable, I plan to have a pack of these in my desk drawer (or in the refrigerator) at work for those days when brewing hot coffee isn't right - but a smooth and satisfying 6.8 ounces of the 'real thing' chilled would really hit the spot.
Label: positive

Text: Not exactly the highest possible praise, but as something of a coffee snob, I was surprised to find this so palatable.  And really, if you need a caffeine hit, isn't it better to down something that didn't have to be formulated inside of a lab?  Five Hour Energy Drinks, I'm talking to *you*.What's in the can?  Coffee, beet sugar, potassium carbonate and potassium citrate.  Those last two you can buy in the vitamin/supplement aisle of your drugstore, and they do have natural antacid properties - I don't think they are used here as preservatives, but rather to tone down coffee's natural acidity.  I get good balance in the flavor of the coffee, so it's easy to enjoy without the addition of milk - great for me as a lactose-intolerant coffee drinker.  If you happen to already cold-brew in the fridge at home for lower acid, you'll appreciate the difference.  On the other hand, if you're a huge fan of bright, acid-y Central American coffees as opposed to the deeper Sumatrans and Suwalesis, then for you this might be a deficit.I'd keep this around in warm weather to offer iced to guests.  It's true that you can probably brew a better cup, if you start with good beans, but I think the convenience here is worth the price, particularly if you don't normally brew much coffee. Roasted beans kept in storage go stale - FAST.For decades, Illy has enjoyed a reputation for reliable, if not exceptional quality, and I'd sooner have one of these than a Starbucks canned or a McCafe any day. (Even if they did partner with Coca Cola for this latest Issimo line, says the coffee snob.)
Label: positive

Text: I enjoy my coffee but I normally add only a touch of sugar, if any at all to my coffee. When I do add sugar, I would put no more than a 1/2 teaspoon of sugar in my cup of coffee. I also will generally choose a dark roast coffee, like French Roast or Sumatra.This illy Caffe coffee drink is an Italian Roast coffee. It has a decent taste in my opinion. I was expecting some sort of "canned" taste but didn't sense that at all while I was drinking it. But I did experience an aftertaste that I can only describe as somewhat metallic.I usually drink the dark roast coffees but was surprised to find that this had a really  nicely balanced coffee taste without the pronounced bitterness usually associated with the bigger, bolder dark roasts to which I'm accustomed.It was the added sugar that I don't really care for. The serving size is 6.8 fluid ounces and that contains 10g of sugar. A teaspoon of sugar is approximately 4 g which means this 6.8 oz can of coffee has the equivalent of 2.5 teaspoons of sugar. Even if I did put sugar in a cup of coffee, I'd only put in a single teaspoon. So for me it was way too sweet. Knowing that many people do sweeten their coffee, I'd say that many people would not find this as overly sweet as I did. You'd probably like it if you sweeten your coffee.I find it convenient to be able to have with you on trips or travels, a good quality cup of coffee. This tastes much better to me than your typical gas station swill that is passed on as coffee. So for trips or commutes it would be a great choice. Just be careful if you are diabetic or have sugar restrictions because there is quite a bit of sugar in a serving. Other than that it tastes pretty good to me.
Label: positive

Text: Through the years, I've tried just about every pre-made coffee beverage imaginable. I am a coffee lover and enthusiast, so I'm married to the idea of buying a can or bottle of a delicious coffee based product that I'll live happily ever after with. My experiences, however, have yet to yield bliss. Some are just fine, but nothing has ever made me say "Wow! I'd like to drink this all the time!" I had heard some positive things about the illy issimo brand, so given the opportunity--I sampled both their Caffe and their Cappuccino.Caffe (2 1/2 stars) is, in their own words, an Italian Espresso Style Coffee Drink. At first, I thought this drink had a strong and pronounced coffee flavor. But something is slightly off. The more I drank, it seemed too sweet and too bitter at once, which is a strange and not altogether pleasant combination. As it is essentially comparable to drinking an iced espresso, it may not be as palatable as some of their other creations. But, it's not really something that I would sample again. And at an approximate cost of $2.00 per can (only 6.8 ounce), this serves more as a caffeine boost than a refreshing beverage. Made with real Arabica coffee, I was also intrigued by the "Do Not Shake" warning which left me with the unappetizing visual of the particulate matter that must still be in the liquid drink (fine for Turkish coffee, not so appealing for something in a can).Cappucino (3 1/2 stars) is, in their own words, an Espresso Style Coffee Drink with Lowfat Milk and Cocoa. So, at 8.45 ounces this is a slightly bigger drink. As some of the harshness is buffered by the milk and cocoa combination, this one easily had a more pleasant taste. Still, a bit sweet for my liking but better overall. I'm still not blown away and I won't be a regular purchaser of this, or any illi issimo brand drinks, but I didn't dislike this one. Nice enough once in a while, but not inspiring enough to purchase a case. I guess I'll remain a fresh made kind of coffee drinker! KGHarris, 6/12.
Label: positive

Text: The first thing I did was Shake The Can. Then I saw the "Do Not Shake" warning but I opened the can anyway and nothing happened. I suspect the DNS is in reference to the Potassium Bicarbonate (fizz-producer?) that lurks within the ingredients. There WAS a nice little "kick" to this -- perhaps from the Potassium Bicarbonate.The initial taste produced an "aaah!" reaction. This is a nice full-bodied coffee, straight from the can, or iced (if consumed shortly after being iced). As a coffee purist who grew up with my Italian Nonna and her old-fashioned espresso pots, it would have been anathema for me to put this in a cup and Nuke it. But I suspect the flavor would not be diminished by doing so.Is this a real Espresso experience? The name says it all - "Italian espresso STYLE Coffee drink". Although it stands out over and above your regular strong coffee, I would say it is a mild but memorable brew, compared to the espresso that your local Barista brews for you -- and compared to mild-mannered Nonna Lucia's "stand-your-hair-on-end" potent brew.The sweetness factor is not cloying. The coffee (made with the better Arabica Beans)is too rich to be chugged. It IS meant to be savored.I'm happy with Illy issimos' Caffe -- take it with you to work - on a car ride -- it would also be a nice addition to any backpack or travel-case, as the can is a nice compact size.  Add it to your favorite cofee liquer-based drink(s), or even smoothies.  Price is a consideration -- my budget will not allow this on a regular basis, but as a treat, as I do consider it a quality product.Bravo Illy -- Viva Italia.
Label: positive

Text: Seriously, there's a 'do not shake' warning on the can and it's there for good reason - the contents are under pressure so... you shake it at your own risk.As a coffee drinker, I can't say that I'm impressed by this canned Espresso to the point that I'd stop making my own but I suppose that's not the point of the 'illy'. No canned coffee can challenge the flavor and aroma of a freshly brewed cup but when that is not possible, here comes the 'illy' and... it's passable.If comparing canned coffees to canned coffees, the 'illy' espresso is okay as in 'decent' and probably preferable to most other canned brands but to call it 'espresso' is a bit of a stretch. It's got 'sugar' for starters, 50 calories worth of it and I don't know too many espresso drinkers that would tolerate sugar in their espresso drink. And it's not espresso-strong. It's a little stronger than the normally bland canned coffee but maybe only half-way between regular coffee and espresso. As for the taste, this is supposed to be made of 'Arabica' and maybe it is but it tastes more like instant coffee to me. Maybe Arabica-based, cold instant coffee.So... if stranded on an island or anywhere a decent cup of real coffee is not an option or you aren't in the mood or don't have the time to make your own, the 'illy espresso' is an option and at least as good, possibly better than alternative canned or bottled coffees. In other words, "It's Okay" (3 stars) if used as directed and your expectations are reasonably low.________________________________Note: Should this be chilled before use? Ice espresso is an abomination but, again, just because it says 'espresso' on the can it doesn't necessarily make it so.
Label: positive

Text: I had heard of this brand of coffees, but had never tried one, until now.  I did not know what to expect, but I think I thought it would be like one of those iced coffee drinks.It's not, at least not to me.  First of all, on the can it says not to shake it.  Iam not sure why.  I had put the can in my refrigerator to chill it for several hours.Each can is 6.8 fl ounces, which is one serving, has 50 calories, total carbs-12 grams, sugars-10 grams, and 155 milligrams of caffeine.  On the caffeine scale I have no idea how high that caffeine level is on a scale from low to high, but by my taste buds I would say it ranges on the higher side due to the STRONG coffee taste that this has, at least to me.I did not find this Caffe Italian Expresso Style Coffee Drink to be sweet at all.  All I tasted was a STRONG coffee flavor, no other flavor was pronounced.That may be because it's an expresso type drink.  Expresso is always a stronger coffee.I do know this, and that is I couldn't drink more than a few sips of this drink out of the can, just the way it is.It's just way too strong for me, but I think if I put it into a taller glass, add some ice and some flavored liquid creamer, it would taste much better to me.For those of you who love STRONG coffee beverages, you may just love this one!To me it packs a real coffee punch for a can of this size!
Label: positive

Text: I like to have a few cold canned coffee beverages on hand, as I prefer them to most energy drinks. Unfortunately finding one that isn't filled with sweeteners has become very difficult.  Living in the Seattle area I was already familiar with illy for their coffee beans that are used in some of my favorite coffee houses. So being given the chance to try some of their canned coffee beverages was something I was looking forward to.After spending the night in the fridge, I opened the illy issimo Caffè without shaking it as noted on the can and poured it into a glass. (*See customer images) The color was very nice, it looked like a strong cup of coffee. The smell was good too and although I knew it was sweetened it wasn't coming across too much. The coffee was strong, very smooth and thankfully not too sweet. I was able to enjoy the entire can in one sitting.Overall illy issimo has a great product and for once I've found one that isn't too sweet for me to enjoy. Plus now that I have been introduced to their canned coffee line I am looking forward to trying this one next: illy issimo Caffè (No Sugar)
Label: positive

Text: I have tried a variety of chilled coffee/espresso drinks, and while I like them individually, I have yet to find any drink that I would go out of my way to try repeatedly. Maybe this didn't amaze me, but I liked that it was an adult coffee drink.I drink black coffee with no sugar - and I drink a lot of it. The good thing about this brand is, like another reviewer said, the coffee is the star. I tried it right after a cup of my normal hot coffee, and it did not pale in comparison.I drank the Caffe chilled and it was not bitter or over-sweet like so many of these drinks. I normally drink high-end coffee, and this tasted pretty legit in comparison. So I think quality ingredients were used.It's a little stronger than my normal coffee - which it should be, since it's an espresso drink.Another review thought the sugar overwhelmed the flavor. It's funny - I didn't get that at all. It IS a little sweet (and sugar is the second listed ingredient), but it wasn't something I noticed that much.Bottom line - good ingredients + adult taste = solid quality.If you're a black/hot coffee fan like me this probably won't change your habits, but for a hot day this might be a good switch.
Label: positive

Text: I have come to expect Illy coffee to symbolize good and consistent quality. I do prefer other brands of espresso, but Illy's consistency is great since it is to be found in many restaurants around the globe.So it is with some sadness that I write a negative review. First off - please note that this is actually a coffee flavored energy beverage made and packaged by Coca Cola. Illy has partnered in order to take proceeds by allowing their name to be used. There might be a some of their coffee used, but it would be processed coffee extract, and so there wouldn't be much reason to use expensive Arabica coffee when extract from lower quality Robusto would work.This canned beverage seems to taste pleasant enough at first, but then it starts to turn metallic. The coffee flavor really is a 'coffee flavor'. There is just something artificial and chemical tasting about this drink. Granted, it's only 45 calories, most coming from the beet extract sweetener.  And don't be fooled by beet sweetener, it's really no different than any other unbleached sugar.  It is regularly used to Chaptalize wine in fact.  Don't be lured in by the low calorie claim: fresh brewed iced coffee without sweetener? Zero calories. Yes. Zero. (okay, actually 0.01 calories! sorry!) So it's not like they're really doing anything crazy by offering this at 45 calories.  It's just less sugar added than the super sweet beverages.  Really it seems the sweetener was added to try and mask the artificial and metallic flavor.There are better iced coffees on the market.  I hope we really start to see some more variety and availability soon.  Other countries like Japan have a huge market for bottled and canned unsweetened tea and coffee, so hopefully we can get on the bandwagon soon.  Hopefully with better tasting results.
Label: negative

Text: I made the mistake of taking my first sip of this coffee after tasting something sweet. It's bitter! But tasting it later I came to appreciate the flavor.Unlike a lot of canned/bottled coffee drinks, to me this tasted like real coffee. Nice and strong, not too sweet. If you put a lot of sweet-and-white in your coffee, you will find this drink unbearably bitter, in that case I recommend the Cappucino version of the same brand.Nutrition facts: 50 calories in the can, 20mg sodium, 12 carbs (10g sugars, 1g fiber) -- which isn't bad. Caffeine content is 155 mg in the 6.8 fl oz. The ingredients list coffee, sugar, potassium bicarbonate, and potassium citrate. No milk, which is healthier, and I prefer that because I'm lactose intolerant. It also means it lasts longer -- the best-by date on my sample can is Sept. '12 (received April).The can lists this nice coffee as a Product of Denmark. I've tasted coffee in Denmark, and I can't dispute that, though I can tell you this method is a whole lot cheaper.I drank it cold and I really enjoyed this coffee. I would really like to get more... but it is a bit pricy. If you are on the go a lot this is an easy solution.
Label: positive

Text: I really like coffee, but overall I would rate this as "okay" tasting - it is very strong, and has a bit of a bite in the flavoring that made me get a piece of gum to get the taste out of my mouth.  After a couple of sips, I was done and would rate it a 3 out of 5 stars.I opened this can up in the office, and passed around small sampler cups to my co-workers to get their thoughts as I work with several coffee connoisseurs.  Three women and one man said they thought it was good, and the three women would rate it a 4 on a 5 scale while the man said he would rate it a 3..Another man who typically buys and enjoys the Starbucks canned beverages said he would rate it a 3 out of 5 stars.Including myself, none of the test subjects hated it but everyone said the same thing - while they thought it was good, none of them believed it was "good enough" to make them want to go out and purchase some.  When I asked why, they had the same impression I had in that the coffee place downstairs was better none of us were really compelled to add this to our regular routines and shopping lists.One thing to note is there is a lot of sugar in this 6.4 ounce can: 10g!  The sugar and the caffeine combined should give you a little kick in the afternoon if that is what you are seeking.
Label: positive

Text: I was bracing for this can of illy issimo coffee to contain a less-than-pleasant coffee experience, with a not-so-fresh, and overpoweringly strong-tasting coffee drink.I was wrong.The coffee contained within my little can had great, pure coffee flavor, with no "can taste". It wasn't overly sweet to me (although I DO like my coffee REALLY sweet. Like, "melted coffee ice cream" sweet...), and I was surprised to see that it looked to be just pure, brewed coffee - no cream added. I tend to brew my coffee pretty strong. Being a "single cup a day to wake me up first thing" kinda girl, I have a tendency to think that my daily cuppa Joe has to be beat-you-about-the-head powerful, and, recent changes in my taste have put that into question, as has this brew. It truly does taste like it was freshly brewed, then left to chill just long enough to give you the basis for a great cup of smooth, flavorful iced coffee, whether you choose to drink it straight, or gussy it up with a little flavored syrup and half-and-half (I opted for the latter).The previous can of illy issimo coffee that I'd tried illy issimo Coffee Drink, Cappuccino, 8.45-Ounce Cans (Pack of 12) didn't really knock my socks off; I mean, it was good, but it wasn't the best I've ever tasted. I'd given that to Starbucks' Frappuccino line of refrigerated coffee drinks. But THIS can of issimo coffee drink really was good enough to give Starbucks a run for their money, for me, at least, especially considering that this brew doesn't contain anything more than coffee, a little sugar to sweeten it, and a few preservatives to hold it over (which any factory-made coffee drink will contain, anyway).If you're on the run, and, for some odd reason, aren't able to get yourself a cuppa Joe on the go, I definitely would recommend this drink for the true coffee purist. I'd be surprised if you weren't pleasantly surprised by it!
Label: positive

Text: I'm a big fan of coffee, so this beverage piqued my interest.  I have tried Starbucks drinks both at Starbucks coffee shops and from the supermarket.  I like them.  This drink is not as sweet.  It seems there's just enough sugar to take the bitter edge off.  When ice is added it makes a refreshing enough warm weather beverage, though I have tasted better.  What the product has going for it is convenience.  The 6.8-ounce cans are easy to store in the refrigerator and can be taken out and drunk quickly.  No process of first making iced coffee.  That's a definite plus.  It is not as satisfying at room temperature.The flavor, apart from being strong, is harsh to the taste and does not provide the satisfaction that a Starbucks or other espresso drinks in a can/bottle do.  As a pick-me-up, the drink is appropriate, since its hefty dose of caffeine will give the drinker an infusion of energy.  Compared to other similar drinks on the market, sugar content is low (which accounts for its bitterness).For those averse to caffeine or those who have trouble sleeping if they drink too much brewed coffee, this beverage is a no-no.  These days, many folks have cut down on caffeine intake, so this product might be counterproductive to weaning oneself from addictive caffeine.
Label: negative

Text: I like iced coffee on hot summer days but since those times are still a couple of months away, I poured the illy Caffe into my favorite coffee mug and microwaved it until it was just the right temperature for me. The can doesn't specify whether this is supposed to be a cold drink or a hot one, so I see no problem with doing this.I also must admit to being someone who only occasionally has espresso coffee, so keep that in mind when you evaluate my review.I liked it.The illy Caffe had a nice, strong coffee flavor. It was just a tad too sweet for me, but that could be a result of heating it rather than consuming it cold. We perceive sweetness differently depending upon temperature and as a cold drink the sweetness might be right. That seems to be the prevailing opinion in the other reviews here, so I'll defer to their judgement.I've tried another illy canned coffee drink, the Cappuccino, and I detected an under-taste of some sort in that one but that's not the case here. It has a nice espresso flavor with no off-flavor notes.The 6.8 ounce can contains 50 calories and 155 milligrams of caffeine.
Label: positive

Text: This is the second of the illy issimo Caffee drinks I've tried.  The first was a cappuchino chocolate drink that was tasty, but a little too sweet.  In fact it was sweet to the point of nearly bitter.My understanding is that the illy issimo line does actually come from an Italian coffee maker, but the brand itself is Coca Cola under license of the ILKO Coffee Intl company.  There is a split personality going on here...the can says Arabica coffee and finest ingredients of the quintessential Italian espresso experience.  But the can says product of Denmark that was apparently put together in Atlanta, GA.  The good news is that the apparently international effort of creating a can of this beverage is actually worthwhile!This one is really good...hit the spot perfectly.  There is a little sweetness to it, but its subdued and deep coffee taste.  In pouring, it looks like Jaegermeister (my admin asked if I was pouring Jaeger bombs).  it has an espresso like foam on the surface (which makes sense, it says Espresso Style on the can) and the smell was like a rich cold coffee.  The taste is easy and flavorful.  Unlike the first drink, I'll actually order a case of this one.  Really good.Pros:  Rich taste, works at room temperature (doesn't require ice) and can very much fulfill a caffeine fiend. But its only 50 calries, only 1% of which is sodium.Cons:  10g of sugar in the can (which is only a 6.8 fl oz (200 ml) can).  But the sugar barely registers.  I don't like sugar in my coffee, but I did like this.
Label: positive

Text: I tend to shy away from cold coffee drinks. I think this is mostly because I resisted coffee until I had to wake up in the morning at the Renaissance Festival and the caffeine was only so successful in waking me. I needed the heat to fully send me into an alert status (and it also became essential in the later weeks when there was frost on the ground and my teeth were rattling). I don't necessarily like the taste of coffee anymore than I like the taste of beer or scotch. I have developed a taste, but that is not the same as having a predisposed desire for it. It's a means to an end and if I am not warmed up in the first sip, that end is less forthcoming.I can honestly say that I enjoyed this beverage. It is just sweet enough to mitigate the coffee bitterness, but not so sweet that you don't know what it is. There is a smooth taste that belies the harshness of the coffee grounds. Furthermore, it is highly caffeinated to the point where my eyes were open wide and I was ready for work.I definitely will buy more.
Label: positive

Text: My first sip really surprised me and I think my face said it all: Strong was written all over my face. I wasn't expecting the taste to be as rich as it was, but honestly with just the first sip I wasn't sure if I liked it. But as I continues to sip I started to enjoy the rich bold taste and could really feel the Italian espresso working it's way through my morning sleepy head. By the time I had the last sip I was awake and my mind was alert and I honestly felt more awake then I normally feel when I drink my other cold instant canned coffee. And illy issimo is only 50 calories and only 20mg sodium which makes my other coffee look like is has tons of calories when I compare the two of them. I normally drink Trader Joe's with it's 120 calories and sometimes I feel like I need 2 of them just to wake up in the morning, but with illy issimo 1 can was all I needed and I was awake. With the difference in calories and how well it worked I think it might be time I thought about switching coffee brands.
Label: positive

Text: I'll preface my review by saying that I generally give 5 stars to products I consider "best of," hpwever, the can aside, it is not the best coffee I have had (I am something of a coffee fiend, and personally partial to African varietals and single estate coffees).  It is the best coffee I have had in a can.  That is not, however, saying much.  The few times I have had canned coffee (I generally avoid packaged processed foods) it has tasted oily, as that is what "non-dairy creamer" is, oil, and the coffee itself was pretty nasty.  This coffee, however, is made by a coffee company, and a European gourmet coffee company at that.  They know coffee, how to choose, roast and brew it.  It is smooth coffee, lightly sweetened, and *without high fructose corn syrup* :)  When I am on the road I generally look for bottled water.  If that isn't available I get lemonade or juice, choosing canned coffee (or a water fountain) if none of the above is available (I just will not consume most of the additives in canned drinks).  This particular coffee, I would bump up in that line, as it is actually more wholesome than most of the packaged lemonade, for example.I would also describe this more as a medium roast, and am surprised, given that they drink pretty sturdy coffee in Italy, that the company describes it as an espresso.  It may be that it is prepared with an espresso roast?  But it is definitely milder than what most people would describe as espresso.  It is, however, reasonably good coffee, prepared with entirely natural ingredients, and that in itself is almost revelatory!
Label: positive

Text: This is a great espresso with just a hint of sweetness.  If you are an espresso drinker this is for you.  If you like lattes, cappucinos, and such, look elsewhere, this has no milk or flavorings, it's just pure, smooth espresso.My preference for anything other than my morning coffee from my Keurig is hot in the winter and ice cold in the summer.  Today's temperature touched 100 so ice cold was the order of the day.  I have a special fast chill compartment in my refrigerator that cools items quickly to 34 degrees and after 4 hours in there I put the can in the freezer for 5 minutes.  It was icy cold the way I like it.  THe espresso is strong and bold without bitterness.  I liked the slight hint of sweetness as I find many coffee drinks to be too sweet for my taste but the Illy Issimo was spot on.  Compared to a Starbucks Double Shot the Illy has a better, more well-rounded flavor.Ok, I raved about the product so why only 4 stars?  The price!  In this age of Starbucks, etc., we are getting used to paying astronomical prices for coffee.  Folks, this is good espresso and convenient in the can but it is still coffee.  I guess we like to pay for convenience.  I could make Illy espresso in my Gaggia Baby for pennies a shot but sticking a can in the fridge is more convenient.  Bottom line, it's still coffee, a very nice espresso, but still coffee.
Label: positive

Text: I tried this illy issimo Caffè Italian Espresso Style coffee drink for a quick energy lift after lunch. The drink is made in Denmark (not Italy) and distributed by the Coca-Cola Co.Likes:>The AL can chilled quickly (1.5 hrs) in the fridge OR pour in a glass and heat it up in the microwave.>Richer taste of Arabica coffee than illy issimo Coffee Drink, Cappuccino, 8.45-Ounce Cans (Pack of 12) without the fat and cholesterol (from lowfat milk).>Per 6.8oz. can: 10g sugar, 0mg cholesterol & fat, and only 50 calories.Dislikes:>Each 6.8oz. can contains: 155mg caffeine and 20mg sodium.  Not bad but for those who are very sensitive to caffeine, even 155mg could cause an extra hour or two of insomnia (happened to me, drank it around 2PM, stayed up until 2AM).>Easy to exceed the maximum daily intake of 500mg caffeine (as recommended by physicians) with just three to four cans.>Competitively priced at $2.01 per 6.8oz can (12-pack) but costs can add up quickly for heavy coffee drinkers.Bottom Line:Tasty drink for an occasional energy boost but not recommended for people sensitive to or trying to minimize caffeine intake.  Give it a try, some may enjoy the flavor and couldn't care less about the 155mg caffeine; make your own informed decision!
Label: positive

Text: For some reason, I had it in my head that this was iced coffee similar to a iced cafe mocha than a Coke that lost its carbonation. I really don't know where I got that idea because the description is clear "Coffee Drink, Caffe".Still, it was a surprise when I poured it into a small cup with ice that it was dark like (you guessed it) coffee.But instead of being disappointed at my unfounded assumption, I went ahead and took a sip and whoa, it was good.Mind you, I'm not a coffee drinker unless one considers a bit of coffee with cream and sugar to be coffee. My expectations were low and yet, this drink was good. Light, not too sweet, but not bitter either. At 6.8 oz, it was also the right size that it wasn't too much either. It was the perfect pick-me-up post-lunch in fact.I probably won't have this too much but I'll try to have it now and again. At 50 calories, it is a much better option for a post-lunch treat than the iced white mocha that I get from a well known coffee chain.So while this drink was different from what I expected, it was a good surprise and I will definitely buy again.
Label: negative

Text: If I made my usual morning coffee in my coffee maker (I like it a little on the strong side), added a bit of sweetener and then put it in the refrigerator to cool down for about 24 hours, I would have this drink.  Pretty much exactly this drink.  The first thing I thought of when I took my initial swallow was that it tasted just like cold coffee.  The sweetness was just about where I like it - not too sweet, but not bitter either.  I can see where buying these small, 6.8 fluid oz. cans would be much easier and more convenient than making your own cold, black coffee if you tend to like it cold and take it with you in a hurry.  I'd down about 3 of these every morning if I could afford them.It tastes fine, really does.  I prefer cream with my coffee but I still thought this tasted pretty good - again, just like cold, black, slightly sweetened coffee. I found it smooth and easy to drink. I like ice-coffee and usually make it later in the day from the morning's left-over sitting in the pot. It has 155 mg of caffeine per can so I would assume it could be used as an energy booster for that lag time during the day around 2:00 - 4:00 pm.  The reason for 3 stars is though it tastes just like cold coffee, there is really nothing to distinguish it as something unusually wonderful and unique.And though I did not see it until after I had done it, at the very top of the can it says, "Do not shake".  Why, I don't know.  I shook it good and hard before opening. I guess they just want to keep bubbles from forming but I don't think the shaking altered the taste or texture whatsoever.
Label: positive

Text: This product describes itself as 100% Natural Arabic.  This is pretty much what it is -- coffee and a some sugar.  I assume that most people will be buying this for a "hit" of caffeine, so I'll compare it to some popular alternatives.Healthy?: Let's face it, most of the other "energy drinks" and caffeinated sodas on the market are chemicals and water. Some of them throw in a couple of vitamins to give the impression that they are healthy drinks, but most are not. Compared to those, this seems much healthier.  It is similar to drinking a brewed cup of coffee.  Is plain coffee healthy?  Probably more so than other "energy" options.Calories: This drink has 50 calories.
