The same amount of cola would have about twice as many calories. and Red Bull would have about 80.  Obviously, the artificially sweetened alternatives to this would have very few.  So other than the artificially sweetened drinks, the calories in this are fairly low.Caffiene: It contains 155 mg of caffeine.  A similar amount of cola has about 20 mg.  This amount of "Red Bull" has about 66mg.  A cup of brewed coffee might have 100-200mg.  For its size, this has a pretty good caffeine punch to it.  I could definitely feel the caffeine "kick in" a little while after drinking this.Taste: I found the taste very enjoyable.  It tastes somewhat like moderatly sweetened, slightly diluted, black coffee.  If you like coffee, you should enjoy this as well.  However, if you like your coffee heavily sweetened and/or with lots of cream, this may taste  a little rough.Overall I enjoyed this product, can recommend it,  and will be buying more.
Label: positive

Text: In a nutshell:  Strong coffee with low sugar and no cream.  Nice energy drink alternative.What it is:  Coca-Cola's foray into canned coffee market (there was one?) in America.  This is Caffe version, which they say is "Italian espresso style coffee drink."  In actuality, it's a simple drink with 4 ingredients:  coffee, sugar, potassium bicarbonate, and potassium citrate (latter two as preservative, probably--although the can says it has no preservatives).  If you count the water and milk (as the can warns that it "may contain traces of milk"), 6 ingredients.  The can notes that it has 155mg of caffeine per can--roughly double that of Red Bull.  Only have 50 calories per can--which is a plus, and impressive given that it has no artificial sweetner.How it is:  Strong without being acidic, with a hint of sugar.  It's pretty smooth, but does have a noticeable bitter signature.  I could not chug this thing.  Note:  the can says "do not shake."  I'm not sure if you should drink it as is, though; wouldn't coffee need to be mixed?  Anyway, if you plan on shaking it, let it sit for a while after doing so; it squirts coffee all over the place when I tried to open it right after shaking.  Lesson learned.  The kick from the caffeine is very much noticeable.  Definitely a good thing to have for a quick pick-me-up.In conclusion:  You don't have to drink Red Bull for caffeine.  And you don't have to pick up a Starbucks Doubleshot (choke full of sugar and cream) as an alternative, either.  I think it's a nice product.Caveat:  Groceries will sell these for much cheaper.
Label: positive

Text: This was a really unsatisfactory beverage in taste and aftereffects. I'm not a huge fan of coffee in a can or jar, having partaken of two widely nationally available brands and found them lacking for a coffee fanatic such as myself (consuming an average of 4-6 cups a day). I was hoping that the Italians (via Denmark, where this product is made) would get it right. Sadly, the Illy Espresso company has failed abysmally with this product. The weird thing was that it tasted fine for the first sip: a nice, bracing bittersweet espresso flavor. The second sip, it seemed a little tinny. Then it was salty. But I was committed to finishing this drink to the full, 6.8 fluid ounce end. The bitter end. Because while I got a faint coffee buzz, I also got major heartburn and a stomachache within a half hour of consumption. I'm guessing as an amateur chemist that adding baking soda (potassium bicarbonate, 20 mg sodium per serving in the drink) with an alkaline Ph interacts negatively with coffee's more acid Ph.  Well, I've popped an antacid, and I will say that this product has convinced me that there are no shortcuts in life. If you want decent coffee, make it yourself. This product contains 156 mg of caffeine per serving. If that's what you're looking for, there are other options that won't feel like you drank battery acid.
Label: negative

Text: I am a coffee freak; I probably need to find a support group.  Coffee is one of my pleasures that I fully embrace and I have no problem admitting it.  However, I am a standard coffee guy.  I don't like the coffee drinks that aren't really coffee, those mixtures that have tons of cream, flavor, sugar, syrup, whip cream, flavor shots and one drop of coffee.  I am a standard coffee drink with a touch of cream and sugar.  Oh and it should be noted I love my coffee HOT.  I am not really a fan of iced coffee.This brings us to this canned coffee beverage that is served cool.  I will confess, I have had a couple of ice coffee drinks that didn't taste bad, if mixed properly and having the right balance of cream and sugar.  Yet this isn't my preferred method of consumption.  Notwithstanding, I decided to give this can of espresso a chance.  After I chilled it over night I gave it a taste.  I must confess the flavor is very nice and smooth.  There is no cream in this beverage, just a hint of sweetness.  I found this to promote a nice balance.  I could tell by the taste a high end blend of coffee was use to make this product.Overall, I am still a guy who will always grab his hot coffee before an ice coffee.  Yet this little can really has a good flavor.  Perhaps it is the fact that the star of this drink is the coffee itself.  There are no flavors or syrups that distract from the coffee, which makes this drink very enjoyable.  In addition, the drink isn't all that big, under 7oz and at 50 calories this beverage is better for the waist than some of the fluffy faux coffee beverages out there.  So coming from a real coffee drinker, I would recommend this drink if I decided that I wanted to venture towards a cooler cup of coffee.
Label: positive

Text: I like coffee, and although I don't really drink it every day, sometimes I just really crave the flavor. I might tackle that craving with a regular cup of coffee, some coffee flavored ice cream or a cold coffee drink.I occasionally like a latte or espresso drink, but usually have them hot. I was interested in the illy issimo Coffee Drink, Caffè flavor when I saw it and decided I had to try it.While I like a nice hot espresso, I have to say I don't much care for this drink. It definitely does remind me of a cold espresso, but apparently I don't much like my espresso cold.I don't think this is a fault of the way the drink is made - more likely it just doesn't suit my taste. I should mention that I have also tried illy issimo Coffee Drink, Cappuccino, 8.45-Ounce Cans (Pack of 12) and liked it better than this Caffè flavor.While I don't necessarily care for it, I think it may appeal to others. If you like strong coffee drinks that aren't sweet and think the flavor sounds good to you, I'd recommend giving it a try. If you prefer a milder coffee drink that is sweeter, you will find the cappuccino flavor more to your liking.
Label: negative

Text: I was very pleasantly surprised by the Issimo Caffè canned drink.  at 6.8 ounces it's about the right size to drink right out of the can or to split in half to make Ice-blendeds.  I tried it both ways and found the cofee taste to be strong enough for my taste but free of any bitter aftertaste or metal "can tatse" that I sometimes get with Starbucks.  With 10 grams of sugar, it is a little sweet right out of the can but I'm OK with that.  I liked it better mixed with some nonfat milk and unsweetened dark chocolate powder dissolved into hot water and made into a reasdy-made syrup I keep in the fridge.  It whipped up nicely and at a much lower calorie and sugar oad than a Frappuccino.I've been looking for a new canned coffee item to keep in the office and at home for some time since Amazon stopped carrying the Cinnabon Lattes in cans.  This has about the same amount of suagr but 20 or so fewer calories so I'm pleased.  I can see myself skipping trips to Starbucks and/or Coffee bean & tea leaf and saving myself a signficant amount of money!  Hey, I like that!  Give it try.  It's pretty much straight brewed espresso with some usual preservatives and of course the sugar.  No added oils or thickeners or colrings like the Crushed bettles in the strawberry Frappucinos from Starbucks - so what you see is what you get.  What you get is a nice, smooth cold Espresso that you can pound down instead of a nasty red Bull or sip slowly and enjoy the taste. Issimo makes canned Cappuccinos as well.
Label: positive

Text: I received both the Caffe and Cappuccino versions and did a side-by-side taste comparison to see which of the two I might prefer over the other.First I tried the Caffe version and was surprised to discover it was just black coffee. I prefer cream in my coffee, so I was hesitant to taste it as it was. But I tasted it and found it to have a nice medium coffee strength with a bit of sweetness to it. In my everyday coffee I don't add sugar, but on occasion it's nice to have a special coffee drink, and since it's been hot here lately, I had poured it over ice as well. I added cream and found it to be quite enjoyable. It's one of those conveniences that one could easily become addicted to.Next I tried the Cappuccino which had a similar taste and flavor as the Caffe, but was milder. I prefer my coffee stronger, so my preference was for the Caffe and still I would have liked it stronger (perhaps this is offered in another version). But the Cappuccino was also good and I had poured it over ice as well. I was concerned about both coffees being packaged in cans as I don't like my drinks to have a metallic tinge to them, but I did not notice anything out of the ordinary. I had, however, poured the drinks over ice into a glass, so that may have helped. Regardless, it was really nice to have sitting out on my deck on a warm day. Hot coffee just does not sit well with me in hot weather.All-in-all, if you're looking for a mild, sweet coffee drink that's quick and easy to take with you on the go or to pour into a glass at home, this is a good choice. It's small and convenient to slip into a purse or briefcase. While the coffee is mild, it still gives a quick pick-me-up in the morning, or in the middle of the day if need be.
Label: positive

Text: I sampled the caffe flavor.  illy issimo comes in five flavors: caffe, caffe no sugar, cappuccino, mochaccino, and latte macchiato.Unless you like iced coffee to begin with, and then iced espresso to boot, you probably won't like this product.  This has no dairy type ingredients, so you will either need to drink this "black" or add something to it.  I tried it straight out of the can into a glass, chilled.  It was really strong, as in very bitter.I softened it a bit by adding some skim milk and sugar.  Even though this is sweetened with beet sugar, the bitterness far overpowers any hint of sweetness.  I added about 3 teaspoons of sugar, and then used about two ounces of milk mixed with this 6.8 ounce can. That made it more palatable.  I then added some crushed ice and poured some over the ice.  Better yet.All in all I would say this is for only hard core iced coffee drinkers.  If you lean more toward a sweet coffee with lots of sugar, this probably isn't going to be up your alley.But if you are an iced coffee drinker and like the bold taste of espresso, you might want to give this a whirl.
Label: positive

Text: You know your drip coffee maker, Mr. Coffee, over there in the corner?  Long cold, the coffee is just sitting in the carafe until you clean it out, right?  Try this: pour it over ice in a cup.  Taste.  Now you know what this coffee drink tastes like without spending the money.Yes, it sounds harsh, but this should have been better than it is.  Convenient, for sure, and the size is a perfect afternoon fix.  But it doesn't taste like delicious brewed coffee.  It just tastes like cold coffee.  There's a difference.  The taste isn't bad, but not great.  It's not flavored up with sugars and creams, which is a GOOD thing...some of us hate sweet coffee that has unnecessary calories.  So, were I on the run, I may pick one of these up if it were cheap, just for ice-cold convenience.  But definitely not assuming that it was something extra special.In fact, surprisingly, it may be helpful to know that MonsterJava Monster Coffee + Energy Drink, Vanilla Light, 15-Ounce Cans (Pack of 12) makes a coffee drink with protein that is really good that I've been buying (cheaper at the local store than the big A).  Sure, it's not especially delicious coffee, but with the protein, it makes a great afternoon slump kicker.
Label: negative

Text: I had tried the illy issimo Cappuccino and found it to have a strong coffee flavor but with out much of an aftertaste, giving it what I assumed was it's "Italian style" coffee quote on the can.  But here with the illy issimo Caffe - the flavor is very bold and the taste sticks around.  Now for some, this is a definite plus.  I think this coffee is designed for coffee fans of bold flavors, dark roasts, French roasts, and especially espresso.  The sweetness is very light and almost vanishes before you can even realize it's there.  It gives you a nice smooth initial flavor, then the coffee comes over the top, dominating the taste.  To me this compares very nicely to Starbucks Doubleshot, except that this seems to have distinctively different coffee bean flavor that is hard to explain as different from Starbucks - but it is slightly.  As for the sweetness, here in the description as I write this - it says 45 calories.  It actually has 50 on the can.  So not for sure if this is an oversight or a recipe change.  Either way, this is definitely something for espresso fans.  If you are looking for a great pick me up during the sweltering summer, this is a nice alternative to hot coffee.  And price-wise, this may save you a few bucks over the price of Starbucks Doubleshot.
Label: positive

Text: The ingredients in this product are: coffee, sugar, potassium bicarbonate and potassium citrate. It has 10 grams of sugar. Note this does not have a dairy product ingredient in it.This is a thin consistency drink with a bold bitter coffee taste. I detect no sweetness at all, although it has 10 grams of sugar.I like the taste of bold coffee with french roast or cofee and chickory New Orleans style or Vietnamese style coffee drinks. This was bold and strong but a thin consistency and bitter despite the sugar added.This drink did nothing for me. I prefer a real cup of hot coffee with half and half to give it a creamy consistency, or I like a strong coffee iced coffee drink or I like an espresso based coffee drink or a frozen Frappuccino type of drink. I love strong coffee that's bold. I usually only use one sugar in a large coffee so I don't like it super-sweet by any means. This is just bitter and I rate it 2 stars = I Don't Like It.There are other iced canned or iced bottled prepared coffee drinks, milk or no milk products that I prefer the taste of such as the ones made by Starbucks (i.e. glass bottled Frappuccino) or the little aluminum cans of espresso drinks such as Starbucks DoubleShot.
Label: positive

Text: For reference, my "morning coffee" is a doubleshot of espresso, hot and very lightly sweetened with brown sugar in the winter, unsweetened over ice in the summer.This morning, I had this, first on its own (ick), and then over ice (slightly better). There is no part of me that's tempted to replace my morning routing with a can of this, easy as it might be.Really, it wasn't horrible. It tasted an awful lot like the other big brand's version of this, which, unfortunately (and I know this sounds horrible), tastes to me like, well, like coffee smells when it's exiting the body. Maybe this is just me, but it's something of a turn-off, and is true for both that other brand and for this one.I didn't really enjoy the texture of this drink, either -- kind of syrupy and thick, when it seems like without cream it should be pretty watery. It was also waaaay sweeter than I would ever want my coffee to be, but given that I generally prefer an unsweetened cold coffee, I'm not sure how much stock you want to put in that.Overall, if you like that other brand's version of this product, I don't see why you wouldn't like this, but if you're hoping for something better -- I don't think this is it.
Label: positive

Text: I had two of these to try. My first impression was that this was kind of small, compared to Starbucks bottled drinks and other popular coffee drinks at the store for around the same price.As for taste, the first one....it doesn't taste bad, but to me it doesn't taste good either. Meaning, at first blush/taste it comes across as water with some espresso in it, not the other way around. I had it cold, so perhaps it's a bit stronger if served at room temperature. Truly, it tastes like they filled the can with water, almost to the top. Then dropped in a splash of espresso. That give it the very slight bitter taste, which is more in the aftertaste really.The second can was what the manufacturer was probably going for.  A stronger espresso flavor. Good, kind of bold, espresso-ish. I'd say the second can was not only much better, but worth buying again.Just a heads-up for folks, this is in no way creamy, it's truly an espresso drink..not a latte etc.It contains 6.8 ounces in this wee can. So,  that 1 cup measuring cup in your kitchen? It would not even fill that, which is 8 ounces.  This is barely a juice box. If it tasted like a real SHOT of espresso, the small size would be more than exciting, even a little endearing.On the good side, I love the idea of coffee to go. If the flavor was much more consistent I'd buy these when I didn't want a creamy, cold drink.Sheri Gill - author
Label: negative

Text: I'm a big ice coffee drinker but I usually prefer to either make it myself or purchase it from a coffee shop where I know it's at least been made on the premise. Freshness is generally something that you can recognize when it comes to coffee. That's why I avoid drinks like those Starbucks Double Shot cans. They taste generic and fake.I usually use a Tassimo pod based brewer to get my caffeine fix but I've made coffee using illy beans before. It really is great coffee. As far as coffee drinks go, it's pretty hard to beat the illy issimo coffee drink. It actually tastes like coffee and isn't drowned out by preservatives or whatever other junk they put into canned coffee.I tend to take my coffee with as little milk as possible. I found that the milk level in this drink was fine, something that I usually take umbrage with when it comes to canned coffee. I suppose you could add more milk if you really wanted to, but I wouldn't bother.The price for a pack of twelve of these is a bit steep for coffee, but I guess it makes sense considering what the other canned coffees cost. If canned coffee is your thing, certainly go for the illy coffee drink. It really does taste quite good.
Label: positive

Text: This is some really nasty tasting stuff. I just tried this one "illy issimo Coffee Drink, Cappucino, 8.45-Ounce Cans" before trying this one. Of the two, this is definitely the worst. But saying the other one was better isn't really saying much because it was really nasty tasting too.  The closest thing I can compare the flavor of this to is a pot of coffee that was left cooking on a hot plate till there was only a cup left in the pot. Then sat for 2 days. It has a nasty burnt coffee flavor with a strong metallic twang to it like it has boiled for hours in a aluminum sauce pan. It left a bitter burnt after taste stuck in the back of my throat and a foul metallic yuck coating my tongue. And neither one wants to go away. It might supposed to have a espresso flavor and I'm not real sure what that is since I haven't had one in several years. But, if real espresso taste's like this stuff, then espresso certainly wouldn't be as popular as it is. Except as a tar remover or something like that. On the can it says "DO NOT SHAKE" and that made me wonder why? After trying to drink it I'd have to say its probably because it still has a bunch of coffee grounds settled in the bottom of it. And if I shook it up then I would have the chew it instead of drink it. I thought about cutting the top off of the can and see what's in it, but decided that I really didn't want to know. I used to call bad strong coffee battery acid, I think this could actually be used for that.The only plus I could say about this is,,,mmmm. There's only 6.8 oz. of it to drink!
Label: negative

Text: I love illy espresso, so when I saw that illy was offering canned coffee I was both happy and disgusted.As a former coffee snob, I was disappointed that my #1 commercial brand would stoop so low as to make canned coffee but at the same time, I was hoping that this would be the best canned coffee available since it was illy after all.This product is a single European cup (not US 8-oz mug) of black coffee with sugar.  Since it is meant to be consumed cold, there is more than your average amount of sugar in it as taste buds are less receptive to flavours as the temperature drops.  When chilled, this is a much richer tasting coffee product than Nestle, Mr Brown or UCC and the sweetness is as expected.  However, when this product is taken at room temperature or served warm, it is definitely too sweet.  I hesitate to compare this to Starbucks as each brand has its own "taste" and therefore its own fans.Obviously this illy canned coffee is nowhere near as flavourful as coffee from freshly ground illy beans, nor as good as fresh roasted beans from an artisan coffee house.Overall I liked it enough that I would buy this product if I was looking for a sweet cold canned/bottled coffee drink.
Label: positive

Text: Right up front: I like this drink, even though I usually don't want my coffee tampered with: No sugar, No milk or cream, No frothing, No cinnamon, No nonsense.This has a definite espresso flavor and is very sweet. The potassium bicarbonate and potassium citrate effectively cut the bitterness that one would expect. I poured the drink into a glass, and there was no aluminum can taste.Actually, since it is based on espresso, I found that half the can was enough for me. At another time I might enjoy a whole can (which is still less than 7 oz.) Even though was cold, I drank it like espresso--one sip at a time.My energy level usually slumps in the middle of the afternoon. I found that by taking a short break and _slowly_ drinking the illy issimo coffee drink, I was refreshed and ready to go on with my day. (Could they have found a more awkward name than: illy issimo Caffe Italian espresso style coffee drink?)But now the true test: Will I actually buy it? Yeah, the next time I go to my local supermarket I will check it out. If it is no more expensive than Amazon, I will probably buy some to have occasionally. (If you are not really out in the boonies, it just seems strange to buy food from Amazon--and I have bought a lot of different stuff from Amazon.)Notes: -->I noticed some other reviewers were drinking it (and it was suggested) when they were commuting or in a hurry. Drink brewed coffee then. Don't rush through espresso--that is just disrespectful. -->Also, on the can, the promise seems to be that the coffee itself is natural, not necessarily the whole product. -->Yes, I know that the energy boost was from relaxing, enjoying the drink, and the sugar, because it takes an hour for caffeine to actually kick in.
Label: positive

Text: This is a pretty typical sweetened packaged espresso drink. It's adequately strong, could always be stronger, since odds are pretty good you'll be drinking it iced. For my taste, it could be a bit less sweet, but it's not cloying against the coffee, and it's a nice sugary sweetness.I tried it straight and iced, with and without milk, and liked it all 4 ways.During the summer, I try to brew a big strong pot every few days and keep it in the fridge for iced coffee, but I like this as an option to keep on hand in the fridge for days I run out and don't want to wait for my usual stuff to cool. It would also be a convenient take-along booster if you're traveling somewhere with questionable coffee or visiting family members who brew theirs like a weak tea. The flavor and strength is much better than the starbucks instant packets, which are also a decent take-along booster.With 155mg caffeine (about 1.5x an average shot of espresso, similar to a good cup of strong brewed coffee), this has a nice pick-me-up with a low pee factor.For my money, it's a bit too pricy for what it is for everyday use. I'm too cheap for coffee at home at $2 a pop.
Label: positive

Text: This small (6.8 fl oz) drink has a nice clean coffee taste, with enough sugar to make it sweet, but not annoyingly so.  If you are looking for a substitute for a trip to a coffee shop for iced coffee, this is less expensive (roughly $2 per can currently).However, making iced coffee at home can give better results (assuming good coffee, freshly roasted, and ground just before brewing).  By that comparison, $2 per for 6.8 fl oz seems rather expensive.A few other brief notes: 1) This is not espresso in a can.  It is an "Espresso STYLE Coffee Drink".  To me, the taste is more reminiscent of a strong cup of coffee with sugar, but not espresso. 2) It is sweetened with sugar...not corn syrup.  The ingredients list is quite short: coffee, sugar, potassium bicarbonate, and potassium citrate.  Not sure what those last two ingredients lend to the taste (they are not preservatives), but I never use them when I make iced coffee at home, and I'm happy with the results. 3) Finally, since I keep an eye on "points" in my daily diet, I note the following: 50 calories, 12g carbohydrates, 0g protein, 1g fiber.  (I wish Amazon would include the nutrition label for each food product it sells.)All in all, a decent coffee drink that is less expensive than a similar drink from a coffee chain, but more expensive than making iced coffee at home.
Label: negative

Text: Coffee preferences seem very personal and heated debates can erupt over the subject.  For me, this coffee is very good.  It's not too sweet -- just the right amount of sugar.  The taste was rich with no strange or bitter aftertaste.  There was also no 'burnt taste' that I've experiences with other brands.  Regarding other reviewers who complain about a strange acidic or a citrus taste, I couldn't taste anything funny like that.  Be aware that the product contains a preservative named potassium citrate, an odorless but not a tasteless chemical.  The vast majority of people have no problem with the chemical but there are a few where it hits the taste buds in an unpleasant way.  You'd probably know by now if you were in that minority because this preservative is common.Note that I like cold coffee so I poured the room temperature can into a glass with ice.  Thus I was tasting something slightly diluted, and yet it still tasted great.  I also should say that I am not a huge coffee drinker, but I do like coffee candy and coffee ice cream.BOTTOM LINE: if I was in a store or on Amazon and wanted to buy some espresso style cans of this size, I would either pick this brand or Starbuck's brand depending on which one was cheaper.  They both taste very good.  If the prices were equal, I would probably go with this brand just to give myself more long term taste test with the product.
Label: positive

Text: I recently reviewed another flavor of this same drink: illy issimo Coffee Drink, Cappuccino, 8.45-Ounce Cans (Pack of 12)... I find this caffé variety to be superior to the cappuccino variety, in both flavor and value. As far as 'canned coffee' goes, this is probably my favorite to date.PROS:- Bold coffee flavor, but with just a touch of sweetness to cut the bitterness. I found it to be robust and refreshing at the same time.- 155mg of caffeine is a hefty boost in the morning- 45 calories mean you can drink this without feeling guilty, and the use of beet sugar is cool (to me)- no preservatives, natural coffee flavor, etc. add another layer of 'guilt-free' to this productSUMMARY: This is my favorite 'canned coffee' so far. I've used a french press, various drip makers, and have a Keurig at my desk at work -- but, if I was in a rush, I'd definitely be fine with drinking this stuff on the go. Right out of the frig it's flavorful, refreshing, has enough caffeine to get you going in the morning, and is low-calorie and preservative-free. I normally don't buy products like this but am seriously considering it after enjoying my free sample.
Label: positive

Text: I should start with a disclaimer that I am a real coffee lover and a habitual consumer of strong brewed Arabica cut with nonfat milk but no sugar. I learned to love iced coffee at my mother's knee - she too loved coffee but in summer when hot brew was intolerable, made her cold glass of joe with whole milk, sugar and then ice cubes.This illy issimo caffe Italian espresso style coffee drink struck me as really strong although reasonably smooth but with some kind of a taste of - perhaps - citric acid. Something "chemical". Something that stays on the tongue when you'd think it wouldn't.I waited for a nice hot day to have this; had it chilled down in the fridge, waiting. It did give me the fog-clearing of caffeine but when I found it a bit strong, I tried cutting it with my nonfat milk. Whoa - the color didn't even alter, this is STRONG stuff. When I got to half milk, it began to have a lighter look, and the taste became more what I like. I kept sipping it and getting a reaction that it tastes somewhat chemical compared to my usual brew. I wanted to like it but don't completely but that may be just me, and partially the effect of it having the sugar that I am no longer used to.If you prefer your coffee without sweetener as I do, please note the manufacturer does produce an unsweetened version.
Label: negative

Text: I drink coffee for the flavor - I typically drink decaf and since abandoning artificial sweeteners 7 months ago, I use real sugar, sometimes cream. Since I'm unused to this volume of caffeine, it was unwise for me to consume this can late in the evening. I was wide awake all night long, unable to sleep more than a few minutes here and there.  It's unusual to get a product in a can that only has 4 ingredients as this illy issimo Caffé coffee. While it contains sugar, it was not sweet enough for me by any stretch - but I like it sweeeeet. Still, it was fully satisfying. My husband is a champion drinker of coffee. If coffee drinking were an Olympic sport, he would medal in it daily... so I let him sample this drink to get his feedback. He said he doesn't like cream in his coffee. I informed him that it didn't have any, but he insisted he could detect cream. I personally think he was thrown off by the temperature - he prefers piping hot, and I had just pulled this can out of the fridge. That being said, if you cannot tolerate cold coffee, you will not be in the market for this product. Personally, I like it at any temperature.
Label: positive

Text: I recently tried illy issimo's Cappuccino (8.45 ounce can) and really enjoyed it. I decided to try their slightly smaller (6.8 ounce) Caffe coffee drink as well, and I came away largely disappointed.  Obviously, there is less here to enjoy.  After a few generous sips, I found the can nearly drained.  I appreciate the fact that the entire drink clocks in at only 45 calories, making it perhaps the healthiest of all the commercially available cold coffee drinks.  I was also pleased to see that it uses beet sugar as a natural sweetener as opposed to corn syrup, and of course, all illy issimo drinks uses 100% Arabica coffee.  So, there's no denying the quality.But in short, this was not nearly as tasty or as satisfying as the Cappuccino I previously tried.  The Caffe was intense, slightly creamy, and just about as sweet as I would want it.  But it left me with a bitter, unpleasant aftertaste that lingered far too long.  You should not feel the sudden urge to brush your teeth or drink cold water after enjoying espresso, but that is exactly how I felt after finishing this.  Therefore, despite the quality ingredients, the initial taste, and the impressive low-calorie count, I cannot recommend this.  I'd rather double up on the calories and enjoy a larger can of their superior Cappuccino.
Label: negative

Text: At only 45 calories per can, it is in a class by itself; at more than $1.91 per can, it faces a lot of competition for my morning pick-me-up, including Green Mountain Coffee Lake & Lodge and SToK Caffeinated Black Coffee Shots.My wife didn't care for this blend's full-bodied flavor but I preferred this over illy issimo Cappuccino, which we also tested for free through Amazon's Vine program. My wife prefers coffee hot and with milk and sugar. My preference is to drink house blends, espresso and Americano black (hot) or over ice.This sweetened, bold ice coffee is best enjoyed over crushed ice. Straight out of the can is a bit harsh (see picture) but this blend mellows nicely over ice. My best advice is that you purchase a single can of illy issimo Caffè before committing to buy a pack of 12.Rating: Three stars.* 100% natural Arabica coffee* No preservatives* OU-D Kosher* Sweetened with beet sugar* Offers a RTD coffee option for lactose intolerant consumers* 6.8 fluid ounces (200 milliliters) per canNutritional Information:* 45 calories* 0 grams of fat* 10 grams of sugar* 155 milligrams of caffeine
Label: positive

Text: I'm an avid coffee drinker.  I drink it black, cold, in a frap...  Illy happens to be one of my favorite brands for making lattes and cappuccinos, so I felt pretty confident that this drink would be good.  I was half right.  This may be one of the oddest things I've drunk yet.At the first sip, it tasted like lightly sweetened, fairly strong espresso, but there was something off.  I took another sip, trying to figure out what it was, but I couldn't quite put my finger on it.I decided to try pouring it into a glass to see if this alleviated the strangeness.  I took another sip and, yep, still strange.  It took me a while to realize that the strangeness was that it seemed almost like it was carbonated, yet it didn't fizz.  This made no sense to me, so I checked the label to find that potassium bicarbonate and potassium citrate are two of the ingredients.  Bingo.  My guess is these ingredients are conspiring to give this coffee a strange, difficult to describe aftertaste.Ultimately, this ruined the beverage for me.  Even though I liked the taste of the coffee and thought it had just the right level of sweetness, I couldn't get past that weird tang and vaguely fizzy feeling.  I won't be coming back for more of this.
Label: negative

Text: I was interested to try this product in making my occasional frozen blended coffee.  I find it a pain to have to brew coffee only to let it cool off first before I can make my blended coffee.  If I don't let it cool, the hot coffee melts the ice too quickly, ruining the "frozen" part of the treat, and then I might as well just have made a hot latte-style drink in the first place.  I had toyed with the idea of making my frozen treat using canned espresso, and decided to give it a try with this.I did taste this straight out of the can.  Even though I am not a black coffee person, the taste of this was average as far as coffee goes.  It was strong, which of course it should be, but I didn't find it bitter.  The bubbles were not like soda bubbles.  Rather, it was like a mild fizz, and I can't even come up with something to compare it to, because all other carbonated beverages I've had have been far stronger in the carbonation than this.I added the can to my usual recipe in the blender.  The first thing I noticed when I was finished blending was the foam.  Lots of foam, thanks to the carbonation.  It was great, actually, and I liked it a lot - it gave the whole thing a real cafe' feel, like I'd made this with fresh espresso.The flavor of my blended beverage was better than when I make it with my regular coffee, more like when I order one of these at a coffee shop.  Again, I'm sure this is because I used more of an espresso-style beverage, like a coffee shop would do.  The coffee flavor wasn't watered down, and it mixed well with the other flavors I had added.For my purposes, this drink did exactly what I wanted.  I will gladly use it again for making my frozen treats.
Label: positive

Text: Okay, maybe it's not exactly unbelievable, because it is exactly what good maraschino cherries looked and tasted like when I was a kid. They are dark. The syrup is thick and dark. The aroma is the essence of cherries and their presence in a cocktail is magical -- taking cherries from a flavorless, if colorful, garnish, to another layer of delicious bouquet and taste.Unless you are over 45, it is quite possibly you never tasted a maraschino cherry like theseWhile the price is not inexpensive, the value, in terms of what is added to any drink or recipe with these, is quite high. Two of these cherries will outpower a dozen of those bright red "things". And don't let cocktails be the only use. Your children deserve to experience what a "cherry" cola or other soft-drink "should" taste like, or an over-the-top sundae or banana split. Similarly, I believe I am going to get my wife to make a Bavarian Schwartzwalder Kirchtorte (Bavarian Black Forest Cherry Cake) with these to really experience the sublime bliss that is cherry and chocolate.They muddle in cocktails extremely well and infuse the entire drink with an exquisite hint of cherry. Old Fashionds and Manhattans become more sublime, and cherries suddenly hold their own with oranges, lemons and other fruits in a cocktail.
Label: positive

Text: I purchased this product after reading the great reviews here on Amazon and on cocktail-oriented websites. Having spent a good deal of time experimenting with different recipes to make my own cocktail cherries, I was eager to try this classic product. I very much expected to love these.  Unfortunately, I found these a little disappointing, and I thought I'd share a more measured evaluation with other Amazon readers.Visually: The Luxardo cherries look great; nearly black in color and coated in a very thick dark red syrup. They're also small; maybe half the diameter of a bing cherry that you might buy in the produce aisle. I think they're somewhat smaller than the commonly available cheap pink maraschino cherries.Aroma: They smell very nice. Like cherry soda, I guess.Texture: They're quite firm. When you bite into them, there's a slight "pop" before they dissolve into sugary goo. It's a little weird if you're expecting the texture of fruit.Taste: This is my big complaint. Yes, there's a nice cherry flavor, but it's completely overshadowed by the fact that they are SWEET. I mean unbelievably, mind-blowingly, unpleasantly SWEET. They are a mouthful of pure cherry-tinged SUGAR. I hadn't quite realized this from reading the earlier reviews, and I was expecting something like a slightly sweetened natural cherry flavor. Make no mistake: this product is candy, and it no longer has any similarity to the texture and flavor of an actual cherry.I purchased this product to dress up our cocktails, but I don't really want to drop a lump of candy into my manhattans. However I didn't want to give up on this expensive product, so I've tried an experiment to try to salvage them: I've poured off most of the syrup from the bottle and topped it off with unsweetened brandy.  I'll let it sit for a few days in the fridge, and then I'll check back to see if they're more palatable. I'm hoping the booze will temper the cloying sweetness of the product.In the meantime, we've gone back to using our home-made cocktail cherries. They actually taste like cherries.
Label: negative

Text: These Luxardo Maraschino cherries are nothing like those radioactive red cherries packed in sugar water. These are real live Maraschino (a variety like Bing) cherries packed in very thick syrup. They have really great wild cherry flavor and are much smaller.The one complaint I have with these cherries is the incredibly thick syrup. For cocktails the syrup is too thick for my taste.The cherries are small, about half the size of the Maraschino cherries found in most stores. There is real intense cherry flavor in these little nuggets. The cherries are all stemless, so they require a pick of some kind for drinks. On the other hand, on top of ice cream, the lack of stem is a great thing. Sadly watching your friend tie the stem up in a knot with their tongue, will just have to be done with other cherries.There is no alcohol in these cherries. The 360 gram jar contains about the same weight of cherries as a standard sixteen ounce jar of radioactive cherries.For cocktails, I prefer cherries preserved in brandy, Morello Cherries in Liqueur and Kirsch Unique recipe Griottines from France 11.8 oz.Luxardo makes some amazing products, their cherry liquor and lemoncello is fabulous. Their canned cherries are wonderful, certainly a step a long ways up from those radioactive cherries.
Label: positive

Text: Great Tasting Coffee!  I only knew Green Mountain Coffee in small K-cups, and never thought about the company and/or quality until I received a sample for review from Bzz Agent.  I now know that this is a great coffee and good company.  I received two bags - Pumpkin Spice and House Blend for review.  Both are equally wonderful.  Both are Fair Trade Certified, and Made in the USA - two great things, - support our planet, and support US economy.  Both House Blend and Seasonal Pumpkin Spice are Kosher, carrying  symbol.  Very nice packaging, and I love the one way valve that let's you smell the coffee.  Great feature if you have someone over and want to let them smell the coffee before you brew it.House Blend is USDA organic, which is great.  Coffee tastes just as it says - Medium Roast, with very nice full body and is not overly acidic.  Regular grind is nice size grind and not small powder that you get with some other brands.  Coffee keeps its taste both hot and cold.Seasonal Pumpkin Spice surprised me by the fact that it is not overly spiced.  I generally do not like spiced coffee as most of the spiced varieties completely overpower the taste of coffee, but it is not the case here.  Wonderful blend where spices are just right - giving you nice taste of coffee with just a hint of spices bringing you in touch with the season.  Seasonal Pumpkin Spice is lighter than House Blend, but very unique and first Pumpkin Spice Coffee that I was able to truly enjoy.  Probably worth mentioning that Pumpkin spice does have both natural and artificial flavors, and artificial flavors are a minus in my opinion, but it absolutely does not impact the overall outstanding quality.  Just as the house blend, nice grind and keeps all the flavors when coldI am now a fan of Green Mountain Coffee and would definitely recommend it as high quality, great tasting, not overly acidic, premium coffee, that can be enjoyed both hot and cold!!! Great Job Green Mountain Coffee!!!
Label: positive

Text: Being at the point of my life when going out for dancing and cocktails with my husband is never going to happen again and going out for dinner is getting closer and closer to the time when we used to go out for lunch, I needed a new stimulating activity and chose trying new coffees to fill that need.Sooooooo, after reading all the 5 star kudos about Jacobs Meister Rostung coffee (ground) and judging that the price was right I hit the "add to cart" button.The coffee arrived yesterday and all 4 packs were tightly vacuum sealed so they're fresh. Good.Opened a pack and the first thing I noticed was that the coffee looks quite dry.Brewed a pot after dinner for myself and a dinner guest so I had someone to share the "fun" with. He drinks his coffee with sugar only and for me it's just cream.We sipped, tasted, considered, mulled over and sniffed our cups of coffee.Here's the conclusion: It has a very earthy taste, not terrible but my taste buds weren't cheering at all. It is also a bit acidic for my taste even with cream or in my guest's case, sugar added. Over all the flavor reminded me very much of the Costa Rican blend coffee that famous $4.00 a cup place sells and I don't like that one very much either.Also, for the first time in eons my stomach felt very acidic after finishing my cup of coffee...can't blame that entirely on the coffee - it might have been from my cooking.Overall this coffee was dry, has a very earthy taste, seemed acidic and will not be on my 'will buy again' list.
Label: negative

Text: I should start by mentioning that, although I have enjoyed a wide variety of teas over the last ten years or so, I am not the most refined tea drinker here so you should probably check out some of the other reviews as well.I discovered Taylors of Harrogate probably 5 years ago and I have loved their breakfast teas very much. I had not come across their China Rose Petal until a few months ago when a British import shop opened in a nearby town (Haddonfield, NJ). I bought it because I loved the jasmine petal teas that I bought when I was in China and I love a certain rose incense, so I figured that I would love rose petal tea as well.When I tried it I was slightly disappointed because it wasn't very strong, by my standards, though I admit that most tea drinkers probably appreciate delicate teas as much as they do strong teas, though perhaps in different situations. I like my tea to look like coffee once I've added milk and this wasn't strong enough for me so I had to try again, this time blending the rose petal tea with a stronger unflavored black tea. It was in this way that I settled on an absolutely fantastic afternoon tea - one cup of water, one teaspoon of China Rose Petal, and a bag of P.G. Tips.Rose Petal Leaf Tea has made an excellent addition to my assortment of tea and I enjoy it in my own way all through the winter. If you aren't as hung up on strong tea as I am you will likely appreciate subtleties that I miss doing it my way. Either way, if you are interested enough in rose petal tea to read reviews on Amazon then I can only imagine that you will get your money's worth from Taylors of Harrogate China Rose Petal.
Label: positive

Text: While I have written lots of reviews on amazon.com I very rarely review grocery items.  But when I find a product that I can enthusiastically endorse I enjoy passing on the information to my fellow Amazon members.  Such is the case with Krusteaz Honey Raisin Bran Muffin Mix.  This product was formerly marketed under the Sun-Maid brand and has been around for about ten years now. My wife and I count ourselves very big fans.Prior to the introduction of Krusteaz Honey Raisin Bran Muffin Mix I had never really seen a bran muffin mix at the grocery store.  You could either make them from scratch using the recipe on a box of Kellogg's All-Bran or buy them at a bakery on donut shop.  Since we never had the All-Bran on hand we always opted for the latter option.  Happily, Krusteaz solved that problem with the introduction of this fantastic mix.  You can whip a batch of these muffins in just a few short minutes and within an hour you will have hot tasty bran muffins emerging from your oven.With Krusteaz Honey Raisin Bran Muffin Mix you get tasty bran muffins that are crispy on the outside and soft and moist on the inside.  Best of all they are packed with juicy and delicious Sun-Maid baking raisins.  Bran muffins just don't get any better than this.  I would take these muffins over any other bran muffins I have ever had.  They are that good!  We generally find that we can make 6 good size muffins from this 18.25 oz package.  Krusteaz Honey Raisin Bran Muffin Mix generally sells for about $3.00 per box and is rarely on sale.  Also, I have found that not all supermarket chains carry this item.  Just to be sure we don't get caught short we generally keep 4 or 5 boxes on hand. I was pleased to discover that this product is now available through Amazon. If you love bran muffins like I do and have never tried this product I urge you to do so.  Very highly recommended!
Label: positive

Text: After reading several recipes for homemade brownies, I decided to play it lazy and go with a box. I used to make Betty Crocker like it was my job, but my husband came home with these instead.Ingredients: sugar, enriched bleached flour (wheat flour, niacin, reduced iron, thiamin mononitrate, riboflavin, folic acid), bittersweet chocolate chips (chocolate liquor, sugar, cocoa butter, milk fat, soy lecithin, vanilla). cocoa (processed with alkali). canola or soybean oil, salt, artificial flavor, sodium bicarbonate.So, the ingredients aren't the healthiest thing ever, but they're brownies, not organic kale chips.I followed the directions (1/4 cups water, 1/2 cup veg. oil, 1 egg). I added some vegan chocolate mini-chips that I had in the fridge. I poured the mixture into an 8-by-8 square pan that I greased with glorious butter.  I cooked at 325 for about 42 minutes.I could not allow them to cool before eating half the pan. I was guilt-ridden but it didn't stop me from having one for breakfast the next day. They have chocolate chips, for goodness sake! And they are so, so, dreamily chocolatey and rich. Perrrfect. I don't like bleached flour as an ingredient, I just don't get it, but they tasted good, which is ultimately the point of brownies if you ask me. If you're looking for a healthier option, don't stop here.
Label: positive

Text: This is a pretty good product.  The brownies came out a little more crumbly than I am used to.  Flavor wise, I thought it was good, especially when paired with ice cream (I used chocolate cinnamon). I loved the bits of chocolate chips throughout, but, it never hurts to add more.  I think I will substitute the oil for something else - maybe yogurt or black beans to reduce overall calories.  The cooking time was spot on.  At the end of the day, I think Betty Crocker Fudge brownies are a bit better than these, but I would still make the Ghiradelli Dark Chocolate brownies if I were in a dark chocolate mood.  I purchased my box at Big Lots for two dollars.  I've seen the product in grocery stores as well for about the same price.Nutrition FactsServing Size 2" brownieServings per container: 16Amount Per ServingCalories 210Total Fat 3.5gSaturated Fat 1.5gTrans Fat 0 mgCholesterol 0mgSodium 130mgTotal Carbohydrate 27gDietary Fiber 1gSugars 18gProtein 1gIngredients:Sugar, enriched bleached flour (wheat flour, niacin, reduced iron, thiamin mononitrate, riboflavin, folic acid), bittersweet chocolate chips (chocolate liquor, sugar, cocoa butter, milk fat, soy lecithin [emulsifier], vanilla), cocoa (processed with alkali), canola or soybean oil, salt, artificial flavor, sodium bicarbonate.Contains:Wheat, milk and soy.
Label: positive

Text: Ghirardelli chocolate is absolutely the best. (It's even a valid reason for going to San Francisco for a weekend -- especially to their Pier 39 shop. But I digress.)- I am a very healthy, conscientious eater who happens to luuvvve good dark chocolate -- which is, after all, from a natural plant, full of antioxidants, and it keeps your hair silky & shiny, not to mention the theobromine and phenethylamine releasing the feel-good seratonin from your brain...you with me? - So, in order to satisfy both sides of my Gemini nature I decided to alter the suggested additions to this Ghirardelli Brownie Mix as follows: instead of l/2 cup oil, I used 1/4 cup of organic virgin coconut oil, slightly nuked just to melt it (you can read about why it's the best oil for you), and an additional 1/4 cup of water to balance the scoop of chocolate whey isolate protein powder I add to the batter, to establish a better protein/carbohydrate balance, which slows down the 'sugar rush' to your bloodstream.  I then decided to make cupcakes, because they bake faster and I wanted a warm one ASAP.  I greased the cupcake tins with butter and preheated the oven to 350 deg., baking them for 20-25 minutes (use the toothpick test). It made ten VERY delicious cupcakes, which at about $2.50 per box is only a quarter for a gourmet chocolate cupcake -- such a deal, yes?  Enjoy! ~
Label: positive

Text: Traditional Medicinals Echinacea Plus is an herbal remedy in tea form designed to support the immune system.  Echinacea is a Native American herb and is one of the most commonly used herbal medicines in the US and Europe.  This tea delivers the immune boosting properties of organic Echinacea in a pleasant tasting blend of organic West Indian lemongrass leaf and organic spearmint.  Echinacea Plus has a serene and subtle lemon-mint flavor that is exceptionally comforting for colds, and soothing to scratchy raw throats.  Echinacea provides a slight tingle on the tongue due to the presence of alkylamides.Traditional Medicinals recommends drinking 5-6 cups a days as soon as symptoms appear and then gradually reducing the dosage as symptoms improve.Numerous clinical studies have examined the effects of Echinacea.  Echinacea stimulates activity of cells that fight infection in our bodies and increases the number of these cells such as the white blood cells and lymphocytes. These immune cells attack bacteria, viruses and abnormal cells, including cancer.  Echinacea has a positive effect on wound  healing, lessens symptoms and speeds recovery from viruses.Echinacea also has anti-inflammatory properites which aids recovery from psoriasis and eczema and may help arthritis.  It also may increase resistance to candida, bronchitis and herpes and other infectious conditions.  Echinacea should not be used continually for any longer than 5-8 weeks.
Label: positive

Text: I recently went on a trip to the Portland area and had the pleasure of seeing a Fred Meyer in the area and when I was there the hotel coffee was this dismal packet that no one who really drinks coffee will tolerate ..so I wandered in the store and saw the Folgers french vanilla and it was pretty cheap for a small one so I was like hmm why not and I swear its some of the best coffee I have had in ages I usually grind my own but for a pre-ground coffee this one is my fave now, when I got home I was so sad because no stores here have it so I checked on amazon and here it was and its super cheap on here too so I am now a happy camper and recommend you try this blend of coffee its really smooth.. I know its french vanilla but it kinda reminds me of maple syrup a bit too.update- 03-2012 I did eventually figure out a place thats sells the coffee and I have actually moved from the area I couldn't find it in.. so no longer and issue but if your having trouble finding it locally go to if you have one your local target..they had it and it was cheap too. so yeah hope this helps :)
Label: positive

Text: ASIN B000LKVT4U - A friend of mine owns an organic grocery store near me, and she's always introducing me to new foods and drinks I've never heard of - usually these introductions don't go too well.  Vruit's Orange Veggie Blend is a surprising exception.Falling into the newly-popular vegetable drinks that taste fruity, Vruit is a bit overpriced on the store shelves for what it is, particularly since it's not alone on that shelf.  Amazon's price is quite good, and with free shipping, you can't go wrong.  Vruit loses points from me, though, because - as an all natural product whose largest consumer base is likely to be the organic, save-the-planet crowd - it bothers me that the stuff comes in single-serving juice boxes instead of a less-packaging, more-eco-friendly recyclable bottle.The Orange Veggie Blend actually reminds me of an apricot juice, although a bit more tart.  There isn't exactly an after-taste, but there is an after-effect, in that my mouth feels a bit puckered and strangely dry after I drink one.  Healthy?  Yes.  Tasty?  Surprisingly, yes.  Thirst-quenching?  Not really.  A glass of cold water goes further and doesn't require me to sip from a tiny straw like a toddler.  I won't be buying these any time soon, but I'll still recommend them to others!- AnnaLovesBooks
Label: positive

Text: [...]A nice mainstream coffee with no unpleasant flavors. Not too mild, not too bold, and not unduly acidic. But you had better make sure you have a large mug, because these T-discs are programmed to make a very large cup of coffee. if you have to make one drink then start your commute, then that's justifiable, but for at-home drinkers, you may get a better experience from less liquid. It won't get so cold and lose its optimum taste-delivery temperature.Why do you want 12 ounces of coffee in one shot? 8 ounces twice, a half-hour apart will be a much better experience. However, if you have these T-discs if you hit the button when the mug is filled to around 7 ounces, you get a nicer drink. Less over-extracted and a bit more bold. If you are looking to quench thirst, drink water before your coffee.As with all the T-discs, you can improve your experience by starting with a warm cup/mug/glass and by never using the add more-water-to-overextract-the-grounds feature. If you feel you must add extra water to your cleverly designed coffee drink, do it with plain water using the yellow cleaner disc, not through the spent coffee disc.
Label: positive

Text: The flavor and scent of Simply Organic's flavoring is strong and true.  It is really a light oil extract, so it also seems to have a much longer shelf-life. Considering that the price rivals many of the artificial extracts on the market--yet is clearly better quality--I find it to be a superb value.I love this whole line of extracts, and specifically the lemon extract.  I use it for both baking/cooking purposes (I often replace or supplement vanilla with lemon, as the two flavors really seem to enhance the overall product), as well as to make organic concoctions.One example: I love the smell of Bigelow's Lemon Body Lotion as well as Tutti-Dulce's Lemon Merengue line, but they are pricey. I find it's more cost-effective for me to just buy an inexpensive vanilla-scented body cream, melt it down in the microwave, mix in some of this extract, and when it re-sets it smells just like the expensive stuff!I am also going to try a new toning mask of witchhazel and the lemon extract, which is supposed to help clarify and minimize pores.  Considering the low cost and high-quality of this product, I find myself looking for all sorts of ways to use it.
Label: positive

Text: I eat a high protein meal replacement bar every day for breakfast, and I have lost several pounds. The bar is high in protein, which does keep you fuller longer. By lunchtime I am usually pretty hungry, but I can hold out until lunchtime without a problem. They are a bit dry, so I always have a drink on hand while eating them. The chocolate flavor is great, not that cheap tasting chocolate that usually comes with diet products. The bar has a nice chew, but isn't overly chewy, nor does it stick to the teeth. They have a good flavor - not the best bar I have ever eaten as far as taste goes, but better than tolerable for sure. These bars are lower in fat content to some of the Slim Fast meal replacement bars, which is why I tried them in the first place.This high fiber bar will keep you feeling full for several hours, and replacing a meal a day with them does create results. Following their diet plan of two meal replacement bars a day with three snack bars will more than likely increase weight loss from the few pounds I have lost eating one bar a day.
Label: positive

Text: My girlfriend (Ash) and I have never made Gingerbread Houses before. After checking out the reviews for the various houses we settled on this one. It was shipped promptly, so we set out to make an amazing house (while watching holiday movies). The first thing we noticed was a corner of one of the walls had broken off. This was easily fixed by smearing some icing on the edges. It held well and you could not even tell that it was broken. The key is letting the icing dry long enough, so it acts like glue.Constructing the house was not too hard. We let it sit and then we started to ice the roof. This was hard. The instructions say to cut a 1/4 inch hole in the bag of the icing, but this is too big. It made gobs of icing go on the roof. Ash ended up having to use her fingers to smooth the icing out. We could not outline the windows, and we did not achieve the thin spray of icing the picture shows on the box. This is the one thing that prevented me from giving this kit five stars.The frosting tastes great. The candy does too and it is plentiful minus peppermints. We are still eating candy pieces. They stuck well to the house thanks to the frosting. The Gingerbread tasted good for Gingerbread. We aren't huge fans of it, though, which is something we discovered after attempting to eat a house.We had a lot of fun making this house. I outlined the process (with pictures) on my blog here:[...]You can see how easy it was for us to construct our house.Overall, we were quite pleased with our experience and plan to continue the tradition of making a Gingerbread House next year. This kit is great for beginners, so long as you cut a smaller hole in the icing bag.
Label: positive

Text: I purchased this $49.99 Gingerbread house kit at the deeply discounted price of $19.99.  I wish I had not paid more than $10.  When it arrived, it was broken in several places, and the candy canes were smashed.  I realize anything can happen during shipping, but it could have been packaged more safely. In addition, the house was much smaller than I had expected for the price. The sides of the house are no larger than graham crackers at the most. The biggest issue I had was with the icing.  The instructions say to knead it to soften it for use. I kneaded, and kneaded, and finally resorted to running it under hot water to try to get it soft enough to use.  Every few minutes I would have to run under hot water again, or else the icing would be way too hard to be able to squeeze.  There is not nearly enough icing included in order to decorate the house as it is shown in the picture.  My son and I made our own patterns as best we could, and ended up laughing about it and simply eating the house unfinished.  We had an enjoyable time, but that was due to our laughter at how horrible this kit was.  I would not recommend this kit. The only reason I am giving it two stars is because the ginger bread was actually delicious.
Label: negative

Text: Like many, I have drank tea for many years, decades, actually, and have had the opportunity to sample everything from the standard bagged teas available everywhere to some of the most expensive hand-made teas developed in Europe using traditional processes. I won't try to pretend this St Dalfour tea is the equivalent of these highly esoteric (and incredibly expensive) teas, but what I can say is that this is a truly great tea for the bagged varieties, and, due to its unfamiliarity, would certainly qualify as a "sleeper" find. I can recommend this particular brand and variety as one of the best I've had.What makes this so good? First, the ingredients are all natural, with no artificial flavors or fillers (check your labels carefully and be surprised to see how many flavored teas rely on artificial flavors). To take it a step further, the ingredients here are all organic (see the information on the boxes and on the website to read about this), with no pesticides, artificial colors, or flavors added. At first blush, one might not be immediately attracted to a "black cherry" tea, but the manner in which St Dalfour blends this results in a flavorful tea that contains a subtle flavoring and aroma of black cherry that is really wonderful.If you prepare the tea correctly, you'll end up with a very smooth and delightfully flavored tea that may become your favorite. One secret for this, or any, tea is to not steep the tea more than 60-90 seconds MAX, and then remove the bag. It might seem like too short of a time at first, but if you force yourself to keep to this time, with a few dunks at the end, you should begin to see how tea can really taste if it isn't over-steeped and allowed to turn bitter. (Everyone has a strength preference and so you'll have to experiment at first, but if you can keep it to no longer than 90 seconds, that should be ideal.) Further, if you have a water boiler that allows you to select temperature, if you use a water temperature right below the boiling point, that smoothness can be extended further.An easy recommend to the tea lover, this tea is one of my favorites. Five stars.
Label: positive

Text: *****Dry Desert Lime is an unusual premium Numi tea. It is herbal tea (caffeine-free) containing solely dried lime---that's all---not lime and green/black tea, not artificial lime flavor, but real lime---alone---a truly remarkable feat. It is certified organic, and is kosher. It has the most amazing tart and lovely taste.  If you like lime, you'll love this tea.  If you don't like lime, definitely pass, as it is a very intense "lime experience".  I love it!On the box, the manufacturer lyrically writes: "From the far reaches of the Arabian Desert, this rare lime has been enjoyed for thousands of years, both as a spice in Middle Eastern cuisine and as a teasan. Traditionally harvested and dried in the hot desert sun, this oasis of Vitamin C has been drunk to soothe just about everything. The first taste brings a distant yet familiar impression, leaving the trace of a fleeting memory on the tip of your tongue. We find that its clean, bracing, and reviving citrus pleasure, nicely balanced with a touch of sugar, lifts the blues. Try it, you'll fall in love with it!"The price is perfect, a big savings over single boxes. If you like Numi teas and enjoy lime, get it---you won't be sorry. If you are new to Numi but love lime, or are curious to try it from the description, I do not think you could find a better source than Numi. In fact, I have never heard of anyone else making this tea.  I enjoy serving it to company, but even more, just for me.*****
Label: positive

Text: I love this coffee.  It makes GREAT espresso drinks, though I prefer lattes and capuccinos.  Lavazza is one of the top brands in Italy (and elsewhere in Europe, having scene their product in other countries). I have also enjoyed Rosa and the pre-ground in the black can (for stovetop espresso percolators).Some people reviewing this product appear to be uneducated about coffee, claiming this is NOT espresso, either because it doesn't say "espresso" on the bag or because it's not a dark roast.  The truth is "espresso" refers to a process of making a coffee beverage, not the roast or the bean itself.  A coffee bean is a coffee bean is a coffee bean.  How you roast it, grind it, send water through it, serve it or drink it is up to you.  Lavazza probably didn't put the word "espresso" on it for one of two reasons: 1) In Italy it's just assumed that if you're normal you're having espresso (probably straight); or 2) Lavazza knows that you may do whatever the heck you want with their beans as long as you keep buying them.Lavazza Oro is a great coffee.  I love the caramel aroma when I first open the bag of beans.  I don't usually drink straight espresso, so I can't comment on how Oro fares as a straight shot.  It seems to make a really nice crema, though that is somewhat controlled by the grind and the machine.  I have a super-automatic espresso machine, and its manufacturer actually recommends NOT using darker, oilier roasts because they may gum up the grinder or brewing apparatus. I've been drinking mainly plain lattes with this coffee, and I think they're phenomenal.  I really miss it if I'm away from home and have to settle for an overpriced, burnt alternative from a mermaid of Seattle.I love that Amazon will ship me four bags of Lavazza Oro every month on subscribe and save, although I've noticed the price jumping up in the past few months.  I hope the price stabilizes or drops back down.  It's currently a real value compared to another Italian espresso giant that comes in silver cans.  Those other guys make delicious coffee, but they're a little too pricey for me to justify buying regularly.
Label: positive

Text: This is really good stuff.  This coffee has a creamy chocolatiness about it that always makes me want some when I wake up in the morning.  I make mine in a 8 cup French press.  5 heaping scoops with a couple of spins in a $10 krups ginder and 3 minutes in filtered freshly boiled water and I am in Heaven.  My mother tells me that this is even good without milk or sugar, which should say something.I should note that some people give this coffee bad reviews because they believe it is espresso.  It is not.  Repeat:  THIS IS NOT ESPRESSO.  Look at the picture or read the packaging if you find it in a store.  It is not espresso.  Amazon listed it incorectly.  Don't give this coffee bad reviews because of someone at Amazon who made a mistake.I should also note that if you want any coffee to retain it's flavor you should not leave the coffee in the bag.  You can always smell the coffee through the bag which means that the bag is not air tight and the flavor of the coffee is dissipating into the air.  As soon as you get any coffee you should always put it in a plastic container that is air tight and stored out of direct sunlight.  Do not store coffee in the freezer because the air cycling will also suck the flavors away.
Label: positive

Text: My cat that can eat fish loves this food! Text: I love this tea. I found it by accident at a grocery store near me while i was searching for "Wild sweet orange". The minute I openned the packet I smell a rose scent similar to that found with Rose essential oil.  (not the scent of rosewater, not the cloying scent of rose perfume nor even the perfumey sweetness of fresh roses, but a scent that is the true essence of roses.)
